instructions: Set milk to heat over medium-low flame in a stock pot. | Stir occasionally to prevent burning. | In a fry pan, heat butter and oil. | Add cumin seeds and let them crackle. | Add besan and roast on low flame until golden and aromatic. | Add chopped onions and sauté briefly. | Then add tomatoes, salt, powdered spices, and green chillies. | Add a splash of hot water and cook until the tomatoes turn mushy. | Roughly chop spinach and add it to the pan with a splash of hot water. | Cover and cook on low flame until spinach releases its moisture. | Open the lid and cook on high flame until all excess moisture evaporates and the spinach is cooked. | Switch off the flame. | Once the milk comes to a gentle simmer, switch off the flame and let it cool slightly for 2–3 minutes. | Mix vinegar and water, add gradually to the milk while stirring gently until it curdles completely and paneer separates from the whey. | Strain the paneer and immediately add it into the cooked spinach mixture. | Switch on the flame and mix well. | Add milk to adjust the consistency & keep it semi-thick. | Taste and adjust salt if required. | Add ginger julienne, garam masala, roasted kasuri methi powder, and fresh coriander. | Your delicious Palak Paneer Bhurji is ready. | Sprinkle freshly crushed black pepper and chaat masala on top before serving. | Serve hot with laccha paratha or bread slices.

(CHOPPED) TOMATO | टमाटर 2 NOS. (CHOPPED) SALT | नमक TO TASTE TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TSP GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (CHOPPED) SPINACH | पालक 1 BUNCH MILK | दूध AS REQUIRED GINGER JULIENNE | अदरक के लच्छे HANDFUL GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी कसूरी मेथी पाउडर A PINCH FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) Method: Set milk to heat over medium-low flame in a stock pot. Stir occasionally to prevent burning. In a fry pan, heat butter and oil. Add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add besan and roast on low flame until golden and aromatic. Add chopped onions and sauté briefly. Then add tomatoes, salt, powdered spices, and green chillies. Add a splash of hot water and cook until the tomatoes turn mushy. Roughly chop spinach and add it to the pan with a splash of hot water. Cover and cook on low flame until spinach releases its moisture. Open the lid and cook on high flame until all excess moisture evaporates and the spinach is cooked. Switch off the flame. Once the milk comes to a gentle simmer, switch off the flame and let it cool slightly for 2–3 minutes. Mix vinegar and water, add gradually to the milk while stirring gently until it curdles completely and paneer separates from the whey. Strain the paneer and immediately add it into the cooked spinach mixture. Switch on the flame and mix well. Add milk to adjust the consistency & keep it semi-thick. Taste and adjust salt if required. Add ginger julienne, garam masala, roasted kasuri methi powder, and fresh coriander. Mix well. Your delicious Palak Paneer Bhurji is ready. Sprinkle freshly crushed black pepper and chaat masala on top before serving. Serve hot with laccha paratha or bread slices. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #palakpaneer #paneerbhurji Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Heating Milk 1:07 Tempering 1:24 Chopping & Adding Spinach 3:18 Making Paneer 4:10 Final Steps 4:51 Plating 5:52 Outro 6:56

instructions: Roast the makhana in ghee over medium heat until light and crisp. | Transfer to a plate, cool completely, and grind the entire quantity into a fine powder using an electric chopper. | In a heavy-bottomed kadhai, bring the milk to a gentle boil, then reduce the flame and let it simmer for 10–12 minutes. | Add the makhana powder, whisking continuously to avoid lumps. | Then add saffron, vanilla essence, and the nuts. | Cook for 5 minutes on low flame. | Add condensed milk, stir well, and continue to cook for 2–3 minutes until the rabdi reaches a semi-thick consistency. | The rabdi will thicken further after cooling. | Turn off the flame and let the rabdi cool down completely. | Once chilled, fold in all the diced fruits. | Add fruits only right before serving to retain freshness and texture. | If serving in batches, mix fruits in portions as needed. | Garnish with slivered pistachios and pomegranate. | Delicious fruit rabdi is ready to be served to your guests this festive season.
raw_text: Get the ChopLab Pro and use code ‘CHOPLAB200’ for an instant discount on Amazon 🙌 Shop now - https://amzn.in/d/bDFH2Jy Full written recipe - Instant Fruit Rabdi Prep time: 10 mins Cooking time: 15-20 minutes Serves: 6-8 people Ingredients: MAKHANA | मखाना 50 GRAMS GHEE | घी 1 TSP FULL FAT MILK | फुल फैट दूध 1.5 LITRES SAFFRON | केसर A PINCH VANILLA ESSENCE | वनीला एसेंस 1 TSP ALMONDS | बादाम 2 TBSP CASHEW | काजू 2 TBSP CONDENSED MILK | कंडेंस्ड मिल्क 3/4 CUP / 250 GRAMS APPLE | सेब 1 MEDIUM SIZED (DICED) GRAPES | अंगूर 8-10 NOS. (DICED) POMEGRANATE | अनार 1/2 CUP CHIKOO | चीकू 2 NOS. (DICED) BANANA | केला 1 LARGE (DICED) Method: Roast the makhana in ghee over medium heat until light and crisp. Transfer to a plate, cool completely, and grind the entire quantity into a fine powder using an electric chopper. In a heavy-bottomed kadhai, bring the milk to a gentle boil, then reduce the flame and let it simmer for 10–12 minutes. Add the makhana powder, whisking continuously to avoid lumps. Then add saffron, vanilla essence, and the nuts. Cook for 5 minutes on low flame. Add condensed milk, stir well, and continue to cook for 2–3 minutes until the rabdi reaches a semi-thick consistency. The rabdi will thicken further after cooling. Turn off the flame and let the rabdi cool down completely. Once chilled, fold in all the diced fruits. Add fruits only right before serving to retain freshness and texture. If serving in batches, mix fruits in portions as needed. Garnish with slivered pistachios and pomegranate. Delicious fruit rabdi is ready to be served to your guests this festive season. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #rabdi #custardfruitsalad Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Makhana Powder 1:45 Instant Rabdi 2:56 Adding Fruits 7:36 Plating 8:05 Outro 9:04

title: Moringa Thepla
description: The Wooden Masala Dani from Cüraa by YFL Home is now available on Amazon. Grab yours now ✨ Amazon - https://amzn.in/d/c9skfWi
ingredients: MORINGA LEAVES | सहजन के पत्ते 2 CUPS | WHEAT FLOUR | गेहूं का आटा 1.5 CUPS | JOWAR FLOUR | ज्वार का आटा 1/2 CUP | GRAM FLOUR | बेसन 2 TBSP | AJWAIN | अजवाइन 1 TSP (CRUSHED) | HING | हींग 1/4 TSP | WHITE SESAME SEEDS | सफेद तिल 2 TSP | GINGER GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक हरी मिर्च पेस्ट 1 TBSP | SALT | नमक TO TASTE | TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP | KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP | GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/2 TSP | CURD | दही 2 TBSP | OIL | तेल 2 TSP | WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED (ADDED GRADUALLY) | OIL | तेल 2-3 DROPS | WHEAT FLOUR | गेहूं का आटा | GHEE | घी 1/2 TSP
instructions: Remove the leaves from the stems, wash thoroughly, pat dry, and chop them finely. | In a large mixing bowl, add wheat flour, jowar flour, gram flour, chopped moringa leaves, ajwain, hing, sesame seeds, ginger-green chilli paste, salt, turmeric, red chilli powder, garam masala, curd, and oil. | Mix everything well before adding water. | Gradually add water and knead into a soft, smooth dough. | Apply a little oil on the surface, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rest for 15–20 minutes. | Heat a tawa over medium-high flame. | Take a small portion of dough, roll it into a round ball, then dust with dry wheat flour and roll into a thin, round thepla using a rolling pin. | Once the tawa is hot, add 2–3 drops of oil, wipe it clean, sprinkle a little wheat flour, and wipe again. | This prevents the thepla from sticking. | Place the rolled thepla on the hot tawa. | Dry roast on both sides until you see light spots, then add very little ghee and cook quickly over high heat. | Avoid overcookin, this helps the thepla stay soft. | Serve hot moringa theplas with white butter, dahi, and pickle.
raw_text: The Wooden Masala Dani from Cüraa by YFL Home is now available on Amazon. Grab yours now ✨ Amazon - https://amzn.in/d/c9skfWi Full written recipe - Moringa Thepla Prep Time: 20 minutes Cooking Time: 15 minutes Serves: 3–4 Ingredients: For Dough MORINGA LEAVES | सहजन के पत्ते 2 CUPS WHEAT FLOUR | गेहूं का आटा 1.5 CUPS JOWAR FLOUR | ज्वार का आटा 1/2 CUP GRAM FLOUR | बेसन 2 TBSP AJWAIN | अजवाइन 1 TSP (CRUSHED) HING | हींग 1/4 TSP WHITE SESAME SEEDS | सफेद तिल 2 TSP GINGER GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक हरी मिर्च पेस्ट 1 TBSP SALT | नमक TO TASTE TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/2 TSP CURD | दही 2 TBSP OIL | तेल 2 TSP WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED (ADDED GRADUALLY) For cooking OIL | तेल 2-3 DROPS WHEAT FLOUR | गेहूं का आटा GHEE | घी 1/2 TSP Method: Remove the leaves from the stems, wash thoroughly, pat dry, and chop them finely. In a large mixing bowl, add wheat flour, jowar flour, gram flour, chopped moringa leaves, ajwain, hing, sesame seeds, ginger-green chilli paste, salt, turmeric, red chilli powder, garam masala, curd, and oil. Mix everything well before adding water. Gradually add water and knead into a soft, smooth dough. Apply a little oil on the surface, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rest for 15–20 minutes. Heat a tawa over medium-high flame. Take a small portion of dough, roll it into a round ball, then dust with dry wheat flour and roll into a thin, round thepla using a rolling pin. Once the tawa is hot, add 2–3 drops of oil, wipe it clean, sprinkle a little wheat flour, and wipe again. This prevents the thepla from sticking. Place the rolled thepla on the hot tawa. Dry roast on both sides until you see light spots, then add very little ghee and cook quickly over high heat. Avoid overcookin, this helps the thepla stay soft. Serve hot moringa theplas with white butter, dahi, and pickle. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #moringaparatha #thepla Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Chopping Moringa Leaves 1:10 Dough 2:26 Cooking Thepla 5:03 Plating 6:46 Outro 7:31

instructions: Heat ghee in a kadhai and roast the makhana on low flame until crisp and aromatic. | Transfer to a bowl and let cool slightly. | Grind half of the roasted makhana into a coarse powder using a chopper or grinder, while keeping the rest whole. | In the same kadhai, add milk and bring it to a boil. | Stir occasionally to prevent it from sticking. | Add the prepared makhana powder to the boiling milk and whisk well to avoid lumps. | Stir in saffron, cardamom powder, rose water, and the nuts. | Let the mixture simmer on low flame for 12–15 minutes. | The makhana powder will help thicken the kheer. | Add condensed milk, mix well, and cook until you reach your desired consistency. | The kheer will thicken further as it cools. | Finally, add the remaining whole makhana, stir, and cook for 2–3 minutes. | Your vrat-special Makhana Kheer is ready. | Serve warm for comfort or chilled for a creamy, rich dessert.
raw_text: Shop Now - https://amzn.in/d/57S1QMy Full written recipe - Vrat Special Makhana Kheer Prep Time: 10 minutes Cooking Time: 25 minutes Serves: 4 Ingredients: GHEE | घी 1 TSP MAKHANA | मखाना 50 GRAMS / 2 CUPS FULL FAT MILK | दूध 1 LITRE KESAR | केसर A PINCH CARDAMOM POWDER | इलायची पाउडर 1/4 TSP ROSE WATER | गुलाब जल 1/2 TSP PISTACHIO | पिसता 1 TBSP (SLIVERED) ALMOND | बदाम 1 TBSP (SLIVERED) CASHEW | काजू 1 TBSP (CHOPPED) CONDENSED MILK | कंडेंस्ड मिल्क 1/2 CUP / 160 GRAMS For garnish PISTACHIO | पिसता DRY ROSE PETALS | सूखे गुलाब की पंखुड़ियां Method: Heat ghee in a kadhai and roast the makhana on low flame until crisp and aromatic. Transfer to a bowl and let cool slightly. Grind half of the roasted makhana into a coarse powder using a chopper or grinder, while keeping the rest whole. In the same kadhai, add milk and bring it to a boil. Stir occasionally to prevent it from sticking. Add the prepared makhana powder to the boiling milk and whisk well to avoid lumps. Stir in saffron, cardamom powder, rose water, and the nuts. Let the mixture simmer on low flame for 12–15 minutes. The makhana powder will help thicken the kheer. Add condensed milk, mix well, and cook until you reach your desired consistency. The kheer will thicken further as it cools. Finally, add the remaining whole makhana, stir, and cook for 2–3 minutes. Your vrat-special Makhana Kheer is ready. Serve warm for comfort or chilled for a creamy, rich dessert. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #makhanakheer Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Roasting Makhana 0:46 Making Makhana Powder 1:20 Kheer Preparation 1:52 Plating 4:08 Outro 5:02

Pour in enough hot water to cover the ingredients, cover with a lid, and boil until the tomatoes turn completely mushy. In a separate kadhai, heat ghee and oil together. Fry the cashews until golden and remove them. In the same pan, fry the makhana until crisp. Transfer both to a bowl and set aside. Once the tomato mixture cools down completely, discard the whole spices and blend into a smooth puree using water as required. Heat butter and oil in a kadhai. Add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Stir in turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, and garam masala. Pour in the prepared tomato cashew puree and cook well until the colour deepens and the oil begins to separate. Season with sugar and adjust salt if needed. Add the fried cashews and makhana to the gravy. Stir in ginger julienne, sliced green chilli, roasted kasuri methi powder, a pinch of garam masala, and fresh coriander. Add hot water if required to adjust consistency. Simmer briefly. Your rich, No Onion No Garlic Kaju Makhana Curry is ready. Add sliced onions and cook until golden brown. Now add tomatoes, coriander stems, garlic, ginger, green chillies, and salt. Sauté for a few minutes. Splash some hot water, cover the pan, and cook until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. Switch off the flame, let it cool slightly, then blend into a smooth puree using water as required. GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 10-12 NOS. GARLIC | लहसुन 10-15 CLOVES SANDWICH MASALA | मसाला 1 TSP SALT | नमक TO TASTE BREAD SLICE | ब्रेड स्लाइस 1 NO. ICE CUBE | बर्फ 1-2 NOS. WATER | पानी A SPLASH Method: Add all the ingredients of the chutney in a mixer jar and grind it into a thick and fine chutney, do not use a lot of water. Taste and adjust salt if required and your streety style sandwich chutney is ready. Grill Sandiwch Ingredients: ONION | प्याज़ 2 MEDIUM SIZED. CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 1 MEDIUM SIZED. RED BELL PEPPER | लाल शिमला मिर्च 1 MEDIUM SIZED. SWEET CORN | स्वीट कॉर्न 1 CUP SANDWICH CHUTNEY | सैंडविच चटनी 2 TBSP OREGANO | ऑरिगेनो 1 TSP RED CHILLI FLAKES | रेड चिली फ्लेक्स 1/2 TSP SANDWICH MASALA | सैंडविच मसाला A LARGE PINCH PROCESSED CHEESE | प्रोसेस्ड चीज़ 100 GRAMS For assembly SANDWICH BREAD SLICES | सैंडविच ब्रेड स्लाइस SOFT BUTTER | नरम मक्खन AS REQUIRED SANDWICH CHUTNEY | सैंडविच चटनी AS REQUIRED PANEER | पनीर AS REQUIRED (OPTIONAL) SANDWICH MASALA | सैंडविच मसाला BUTTER | मक्खन AS REQUIRED Method: Prepare the Filling: Finely chop onion, capsicum, red bell pepper and sweet corn (using a string chopper or knife) and transfer to a mixing bowl. Add sandwich chutney, oregano, red chilli flakes, sandwich masala, and grated cheese. Mix well to make the filling. If you are not assembling immediately, add seasoning later to avoid the filling becoming watery. Heat a grill pan or tawa on medium flame. If unsoaked, cook for 2–3 whistles. Once cooked, switch off the flame, let the cooker depressurize naturally open and check the dal, set aside without discarding the cooking liquid. Heat oil in a kadhai. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add the chopped onions and cook until they turn golden brown. Lower the flame, add turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, and kanda lahsun masala. Splash in a little hot water and cook briefly to prevent burning. Add the boiled dal, mix well and let it simmer gently. Once the dal thickens, mash it slightly with the back of a ladle to release more starch and make it creamier. Crush the garlic cloves and green chilli into a rough paste using a mortar and pestle. Heat oil in a small pan. Add the garlic–chilli paste and sauté until golden. Add a pinch of cumin seeds, asafoetida, and Kashmiri red chilli powder. Stir briefly and immediately pour this tadka over the simmering dal. Stir the dal well, taste, and adjust salt if required. Garnish with freshly chopped coriander leaves. Serve the Akkha Masoor hot with bhakri or steamed rice for a wholesome meal. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #akkhamasoor Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Boiling Dal 1:19 Tempering 2:05 Prep for 2nd Tempering 4:19 2nd Tempering 4:51 Plating 5:40 Outro 6:57

title: Drumstick Soup
ingredients: DRUMSTICKS | सहजन की फलियां 4 NOS. | ONION | प्याज़ 1 NO. | GARLIC | लहसुन 8 CLOVES | GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH | GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. | CORIANDER STEMS | धनिये की डंठल A HANDFUL (SHOWING) | BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 1 NO. | CLOVES | लौंग 2 NOS. | CINNAMON | दालचीनी 2 INCH | SALT | नमक A PINCH | HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED | FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL | MINT | पुदीना 8–10 LEAVES | HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED | SALT | नमक TO TASTE | BLACK SALT | काला नमक A PINCH | ROASTED CUMIN POWDER | भुना जीरा पाउडर A PINCH | BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च A PINCH | LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF HALF A LEMON
instructions: Lightly peel the drumsticks and cut them into pieces. | Add the drumsticks into a kadhai, along with onion, ginger, garlic, green chillies, coriander stems, bay leaf, cloves, cinnamon, a pinch of salt, and enough hot water to cover. | Bring it to a boil, then cover and cook for 5–6 minutes or until the drumsticks are tender. | Once cooked, discard the whole spices. | Strain the mixture, keeping the stock aside. | Let the solids cool slightly, then blend them along with some fresh coriander & mint using a little of the reserved stock until smooth. | Strain the blended mixture back into the kadhai along with the remaining stock, only the woody fibres of the drumsticks will stay back in the sieve. | Add hot water to adjust the soup’s consistency as per your preference. | Bring it to a gentle simmer, then season with salt, black salt, roasted cumin powder, black pepper & lemon juice. | Serve the drumstick soup hot for a nourishing, flavourful boost.

GARLIC | लहसुन 8 CLOVES GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. CORIANDER STEMS | धनिये की डंठल A HANDFUL (SHOWING) BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 1 NO. CLOVES | लौंग 2 NOS. CINNAMON | दालचीनी 2 INCH SALT | नमक A PINCH HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL MINT | पुदीना 8–10 LEAVES HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED SALT | नमक TO TASTE BLACK SALT | काला नमक A PINCH ROASTED CUMIN POWDER | भुना जीरा पाउडर A PINCH BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च A PINCH LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF HALF A LEMON Method: Lightly peel the drumsticks and cut them into pieces. Add the drumsticks into a kadhai, along with onion, ginger, garlic, green chillies, coriander stems, bay leaf, cloves, cinnamon, a pinch of salt, and enough hot water to cover. Bring it to a boil, then cover and cook for 5–6 minutes or until the drumsticks are tender. Once cooked, discard the whole spices. Strain the mixture, keeping the stock aside. Let the solids cool slightly, then blend them along with some fresh coriander & mint using a little of the reserved stock until smooth. Strain the blended mixture back into the kadhai along with the remaining stock, only the woody fibres of the drumsticks will stay back in the sieve. Add hot water to adjust the soup’s consistency as per your preference. Bring it to a gentle simmer, then season with salt, black salt, roasted cumin powder, black pepper & lemon juice. Until then you can make the sweet corn paste & also the green chutney. You can also set the steamer after that & grease the dhokla trays with some oil. Add the sweet corn into a mixer jar along with green chilli & ginger & grind it into a paste. Once the batter has rest, it will thicken up as the rawa absorbs water, add the prepared sweet corn paste along with some more sweet corn, turmeric, red chilli flakes, oregano & oil, mix well. Once the steamer is ready & you've greased the dhokla trays, add eno into the batter along with a splash of water & mix really well, then pour the batter into the trays equally, tap them gently & add a pinch of red chilli flakes, oregano & black pepper from the top. Set a tadka pan over high heat, add oil & let it get hot. Then add the mustard seeds, green chillies & asafoetida, stir well & cook briefly, lastly add the curry leaves & pour the tempering over the dhokla equally, garnish with some fresh coriander. Your delicious & instant Sweet Corn Dhokla is ready. title: Veg Korean Corn Dog

raw_text: Full written recipe - Veg Korean Corn Dog Prep time: 10-15 minutes Cooking time: 35-40 minutes Serves: 6-8 people Ingredients: LUKEWARM WATER | गुनगुना पानी 1 CUP / 240 ML SUGAR | शक्कर 1 TSP / 5 GRAMS INSTANT DRY YEAST | इंस्टेंट ड्राई यीस्ट 1 TBSP / 8 GRAMS REFINED FLOUR | मैदा 240 GRAMS / 1 CUP + 2/3 CUP POTATO | आलू 3 MEDIUM SIZED. MOZZARELLA CHEESE BLOCK | मोज़्ज़रेल्ला ब्लॉक 200 GRAMS PANKO BREADCRUMBS | पैंको ब्रेड क्रंब्स AS REQUIRED (for coating) Toppings SALT | नमक CASTER SUGAR | कैस्टर शुगर MAYONNAISE | मेयोनेज़ MUSTARD SAUCE | मस्टर्ड सॉस KETCHUP | केचप Method: Start by preparing the batter. In a bowl, add lukewarm water, sugar, and instant dry yeast. Stir well and let it sit for 5–10 minutes or until the yeast becomes foamy, this is a good way to check if the yeast that you are using is still active. Once the yeast is activated, take a large mixing bowl, pour in the yeast water and add refined flour. Mix everything well until you get a smooth, sticky batter. Cover it and keep it in a warm place to let it rise until it doubles in volume. While the batter is resting, peel and cut the potatoes into small dices, blanch them in boiling water for exactly 3 minutes, then drain and transfer onto a tissue-lined tray. Set them aside to cool completely. Next, cut the mozzarella cheese block into long rectangular sticks or use cubes if that's more convenient, the shape won’t matter much since they’ll be fully coated. Skewer the cheese sticks on a bamboo skewer carefully and keep them ready. After the batter has risen, give it a quick mix. You’ll notice it’s stretchy and stringy, that’s due to the gluten formation. The batter is now ready to use. Before frying, set up your coating station. You’ll need the diced potatoes, panko breadcrumbs, prepared batter, and skewered cheese sticks. Also, heat oil in a kadhai or deep pan to 175°C. Now, take a cheese skewer and dip it fully into the batter. Lift, pull gently, and rotate to coat evenly. Immediately roll the coated stick in diced potatoes, press lightly so the pieces stick well. Finally, coat the entire thing in panko breadcrumbs. Carefully drop the prepared corn dog into the hot oil and fry until it turns golden brown and crispy. Avoid stirring to prevent the coating from loosening or the cheese from oozing out. Once fried, take it out and let it drain over a wire rack or a sieve. To finish, sprinkle a pinch of salt and caster sugar on top. Drizzle mayonnaise, mustard sauce, and ketchup as per your liking. You can also try other topping ideas like sweet chilli sauce, spicy mayo, or even a sprinkle of peri peri seasoning. Serve the Veg Korean Corn Dogs hot and crispy. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #koreancorndog Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Batter 1:19 Prepping Potatoes 4:01 Cutting Mozzarella Sticks 5:14 Checking the batter 6:32 Set-up for Coating 7:52 Coating & Frying 8:05 Plating 9:22 Outro 10:25

title: Stuffed Mushroom Tikka

(CHOPPED) | MINT | पुदीना A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) | FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) | CARDAMON POWDER | इलाइची पाउडर A PINCH | CHAAT MASALA | चाट मसाला A PINCH | POWDERED SUGAR | पीसी हुई शक्कर 1 TSP | AJWAIN | अजवाइन A PINCH | ONION | प्याज़ AS REQUIRED (DICED) | CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च AS REQUIRED (DICED) | GHEE | घी 1 TSP | BUTTER | मक्खन (MELTED) | BUTTER | मक्खन (MELTED)
instructions: In a large bowl add hung curd along with all the remaining ingredients of the marinade & whisk it into a smooth marinade. | To give the marinade a smoky flavour, place a small bowl with live charcoal inside it, add a spoonful of mustard oil, cover & let the marinade smoke for 4-5 minutes, then uncover & discard the charcoal carefully. | Mix well & your tandoori marinade is ready. | Prep the mushrooms by plucking the stem out & scrape the insides to form a cup. | Try using medium sized mushrooms for this recipe. | For the stuffing, grate & add the paneer & the cheese in a bowl & add all the remaining ingredients & mix well, taste & adjust seasoning if required. | Stuff the mushrooms with the stuffing using a spoon, make sure you press the stuffing inside firmly to lock it inside, otherwise the stuffing might fall out while coating with the marinade. | I like to use bamboo skewers to make any kind of tikkas, just soak them in water for 30 minutes so that they don't burn while cooking. | Skewer a dice of onion & capsicum onto the skewer, then coat the mushroom & skewer it, you can skewer about 4-5 mushrooms on one skewer, in the end you can again add a dice of capsicum & onion, ready as many skewers as required. | Do not coat the mushroom with the marinade & let it rest, the mushroom will start oozing out water which will make the marinade drip. | To cook them on a pan, heat the pan over high flame & then add very little ghee or oil. | Once the ghee heats up, place the skewers 3-4 at a time if you have a large enough pan, cook the tikkas over medium high flame, until golden brown from one side then turn & cook them similarly on the other side, let them get a little charred from each side. | Your delicious stuffed mushrooms tikkas are ready using a pan. | The second method is cooking the tikka over direct flame, just hold the skewer over direct flame & rotate it at regular intervals until the marinade gets cooked, it will turn brown from some spots & get charred beautifully over direct flame, you can apply some melted butter & continue cooking it over flame. | This method works best if you want to replicate the perfect texture similar to a tandoor, however it is a bit time consuming. | To cook it in an air-fryer or anything oven, preheat the airfyer or oven at 200°C, place the skewers onto the tray & cook them for 10-12 minutes. | After 5-6 minutes, apply some butter on the tikkas & continue to cook them. | These are the 3 ways in which you can cook the Stuffed Mushroom Tikkas & all of them taste absolutely delicious, serve it with some green chutney & garnish with some lemon juice & chaat masala.

(CHOPPED) MINT | पुदीना A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) CARDAMON POWDER | इलाइची पाउडर A PINCH CHAAT MASALA | चाट मसाला A PINCH POWDERED SUGAR | पीसी हुई शक्कर 1 TSP AJWAIN | अजवाइन A PINCH Veggies for skewering ONION | प्याज़ AS REQUIRED (DICED) CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च AS REQUIRED (DICED) Cooking on pan GHEE | घी 1 TSP Cooking over direct flame BUTTER | मक्खन (MELTED) Cooking in air-fryer BUTTER | मक्खन (MELTED) Method: In a large bowl add hung curd along with all the remaining ingredients of the marinade & whisk it into a smooth marinade. To give the marinade a smoky flavour, place a small bowl with live charcoal inside it, add a spoonful of mustard oil, cover & let the marinade smoke for 4-5 minutes, then uncover & discard the charcoal carefully. Mix well & your tandoori marinade is ready. Prep the mushrooms by plucking the stem out & scrape the insides to form a cup. Try using medium sized mushrooms for this recipe. For the stuffing, grate & add the paneer & the cheese in a bowl & add all the remaining ingredients & mix well, taste & adjust seasoning if required. Stuff the mushrooms with the stuffing using a spoon, make sure you press the stuffing inside firmly to lock it inside, otherwise the stuffing might fall out while coating with the marinade. I like to use bamboo skewers to make any kind of tikkas, just soak them in water for 30 minutes so that they don't burn while cooking. Skewer a dice of onion & capsicum onto the skewer, then coat the mushroom & skewer it, you can skewer about 4-5 mushrooms on one skewer, in the end you can again add a dice of capsicum & onion, ready as many skewers as required. Do not coat the mushroom with the marinade & let it rest, the mushroom will start oozing out water which will make the marinade drip. To cook them on a pan, heat the pan over high flame & then add very little ghee or oil. Once the ghee heats up, place the skewers 3-4 at a time if you have a large enough pan, cook the tikkas over medium high flame, until golden brown from one side then turn & cook them similarly on the other side, let them get a little charred from each side. Your delicious stuffed mushrooms tikkas are ready using a pan. The second method is cooking the tikka over direct flame, just hold the skewer over direct flame & rotate it at regular intervals until the marinade gets cooked, it will turn brown from some spots & get charred beautifully over direct flame, you can apply some melted butter & continue cooking it over flame. This method works best if you want to replicate the perfect texture similar to a tandoor, however it is a bit time consuming. To cook it in an air-fryer or anything oven, preheat the airfyer or oven at 200°C, place the skewers onto the tray & cook them for 10-12 minutes. After 5-6 minutes, apply some butter on the tikkas & continue to cook them. instructions: Sieve & add the gram flour into a large bowl along with salt, carom seeds & baking soda, mix all the dry ingredients. | Further add water & start whisking the batter, don’t add too much water at once, only add a little amount initially & whisk until all the lumps are gone. | Further add more water & adjust the consistency, the batter should be slightly thin but not completely runny, once the batter is ready cover & set it aside & let it rest until you make the other components. | For the thecha, in a pan, dry roast the garlic, green chilli & coriander stems over high flame until they get slightly charred then add the cumin seeds & oil, stir well & roast briefly. | Transfer the mixture into a bowl & let it cool down completely, then add it into a a coarse paste using a mortar pestle along with some fresh coriander & salt, your thecha is ready. | To make the instant sweet tamarind chutney, set a stock pot with boiling water over high flame & then add the tamarind, let it boil for 5-6 minutes or until it softens completely, then switch off the flame & let it cool down completely. | Transfer it to a mixer jar & add sugar with salt & blend it into a fine chutney, then strain & transfer it into a bowl, your instant sweet tamarind chutney is ready. | To make the dry garlic chutney you need to make “chura” first, chura is basically the extra crispy bits of the besan raw_text: Full written recipe - Mumbai’s Famous Bhajji Pav Prep time: 5-10 minutes Cooking time: 20-25 minutes Serves: 5-6 people Ingredients: Besan Batter GRAM FLOUR | बेसन 1.5 CUPS BAKING SODA | बेकिंग सोडा A PINCH SALT | नमक TO TASTE AJWAIN | अजवाइन A PINCH (CRUSHED) WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED Thecha GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 10-12 NOS. GARLIC | लहसुन 15-20 CLOVES FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरे धनिये के डंठल A HANDFUL CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A PINCH OIL | तेल 1 TSP FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH Quick Imli Chutney WATER | पानी 1.5 CUPS (BOILING) TAMARIND | इमली A LEMON SIZED SUGAR | शक्कर 1/2 CUP SALT | नमक A PINCH Dry Garlic Chutney GARLIC | लहसुन 8-10 CLOVES (WITH SKIN) KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1.5-2 TBSP SALT | नमक TO TASTE Fried Green Chilli GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च AS REQUIRED SALT | नमक A PINCH Frying Bhajiya WATER | पानी A SPLASH POTARO | आलू 5 MEDIUM SIZED or AS REQUIRED Method: Sieve & add the gram flour into a large bowl along with salt, carom seeds & baking soda, mix all the dry ingredients. Further add water & start whisking the batter, don’t add too much water at once, only add a little amount initially & whisk until all the lumps are gone. Further add more water & adjust the consistency, the batter should be slightly thin but not completely runny, once the batter is ready cover & set it aside & let it rest until you make the other components. For the thecha, in a pan, dry roast the garlic, green chilli & coriander stems over high flame until they get slightly charred then add the cumin seeds & oil, stir well & roast briefly. Transfer the mixture into a bowl & let it cool down completely, then add it into a a coarse paste using a mortar pestle along with some fresh coriander & salt, your thecha is ready. To make the instant sweet tamarind chutney, set a stock pot with boiling water over high flame & then add the tamarind, let it boil for 5-6 minutes or until it softens completely, then switch off the flame & let it cool down completely. Transfer it to a mixer jar & add sugar with salt & blend it into a fine chutney, then strain & transfer it into a bowl, your instant sweet tamarind chutney is ready. To make the dry garlic chutney you need to make “chura” first, chura is basically the extra crispy bits of the besan batter that are fried separately. Set oil in a kadhai on high heat & let it get hot, then take the besan batter & give a mix, dip your fingers in the batter & randomly drizzle & drop in hot oil, deep fry on medium high heat until its crisp & golden brown in colour, make sure that the chura is not too thick. Sieve it & keep aside to cool down. You need about 2 cups of the chura for the chutney. You also need fried garlic, first pick the garlic cloves with toothpick, place them in a spider & deep fry briefly for 30-60 seconds, keep dipping & removing the cloves, do not fry them continuously as they then to splutter, once fried remove & keep aside. Add the chura in a food processor, along with fried garlic, salt & red chilli powder, use pulse mode & grind into a coarse dry chutney. Your dry red garlic chutney is ready; you can also store in an airtight container & store in the fridge for up to a week. For the fried green chillies, take spicy green chillies as required & put a small cut into the chillies using a knife so that they don’t splutter a lot in oil, then similar to garlic, add the chillies into a spider & fry them by dipping into the hot oil, then transfer them into a bowl & add salt, mix well. For the perfect bhajiya. Check the batter once, the batter tends to thicken slightly after resting, so add water if required to adjust the consistency. Take washed potatoes & slice them thinly using a mandolin. Dip the slices into the prepared besan batter, ensuring each slice has a thin, even coating. Carefully drop the coated slices into hot oil & fry until golden & crisp. Transfer them to a sieve to drain excess oil. Fry in batches & only as needed to retain freshness & crunch. Cut the pav & apply a layer of spicy thecha on one side. Add sweet tamarind chutney on the other side, followed by a sprinkle of dry red garlic chutney. Stack the hot, crispy bhajiyas inside the pav, garnish with a little more dry garlic chutney on top, & finish with a fried green chilli on the side or tucked in. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #bhajji #bhajiya #bhajiyapav Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Batter 2:03 Thecha 3:31 Sweet Tamarind Chutney 5:22 Dry Red Garlic Chutney 6:36 Fried Green Chilli 8:09 Adjusting Batter 8:51 Bhajji 8:59 Plating & Assembly 10:35 Outro 12:52

SALT | नमक TO TASTE TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TSP GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/2 TSP KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP BLACK PEPPER POWDER | काली मिर्च पाउडर 1/4 TSP CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर 1/2 TSP BLACK SALT | काला नमक 1 TSP DRY MANGO POWDER | आमचूर पाउडर 1 TSP WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF A LEMON FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) MINT LEAVES | पुदीना A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) Method: Wash & soak the chickpeas overnight or you also get soaked chickpeas in the market, you need 2 cups of soaked chickpeas for this recipe. Add the ginger, garlic & green chilli in a small mixer jar along with some water & blend it into a fine paste. Now in a pressure add the soaked chickpeas after discarding the water, roughly cut the tomato into quarters & then add salt along with all the powdered spices followed by water, add about 3 litres of water & stir well. Next, put the lid on & pressure cook the chickpeas over medium high flame for 4-5 whistles then switch off the flame & let it depressurize naturally. Then check the whistle for any excess pressure & open the lid stir well. Check if the chickpeas are cooked by pressing one between your fingers. At this stage, stir well, switch on the flame to high and bring the soup to a simmer. Taste & adjust seasoning if required. Lastly add some chopped fresh coriander & mint. Your delicious, no oil healthy chana soup is ready, serve it hot, you can add boiled chickepeas as per your preference and optionally you can add a small cube of butter and some chaat masala. title: Corn Capsicum Masala

instructions: Chop the onions & tomato separately using a string chopper or using a knife according to your convenience. | Finely chop the ginger, garlic & green chilli as well. | Set a kadhai over high flame, add ghee & oil, allow it to heat up as well. | Then, add all the whole spices & let them crackle for a few seconds & then add the chopped onions, stir well & cook over high flame until the onions turn golden brown. | Once the onions turn light golden brown, add the chopped ginger garlic & green chilli, stir & continue to cook until the onions turn completely golden brown. | Then lower the flame & add all the powdered spices along with a splash of hot water, stir well & cook over high flame for 2-3 minutes. | Next, add the chopped tomatoes along with whisked curd, cashew paste & salt, stir well & cook over high flame until the gravy turns crumbly & all the ghee gets separated. | Once the gravy is cooked, add hot water to adjust the consistency of the gravy, it should be semi-thick and not too thin, then add the sweet corn, stir well, cover & cook over low flame for 4-5 minutes. | Then take the lid off & add the diced capsicum, stir well & cook for 2-3 minutes. | Taste & adjust salt if required & lastly add kasuri methi powder, garam masala & fresh coriander. | Your delicious, quick & easy Corn Capsicum Masala is ready, serve hot with some tandoori rotis or other Indian bread of your choice.
raw_text: Full written recipe - Corn Capsicum Masala Prep time: 5-10 minutes Cooking time: 25-30 minutes Serves: 4-5 people Ingredients: ONION | पयाज़ 3 NOS. TOMATO | टमाटर 2 NOS. GARLIC | लहसुन 10-12 CLOVES GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 2 MEDIUM SIZED. (MEDIUM DICES) SWEET CORN | स्वीट कॉर्न 2 CUPS GHEE | घी 2 TBSP OIL | तेल 1 TBSP CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलाइची 2 NOS. CINNAMON | दालचीनी 1 INCH BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 NOS. KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP SPICY RED CHILLI POWDER | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TBSP TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर ½ TSP HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED CURD | दही 1/2 CUP (WHISKED) CASHEW PASTE | काजू की पेस्ट OF 10-12 NOS. SALT | नमक TO TASTE HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी कसूरी मेथी का पाउडर A PINCH GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A LARGE PINCH FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) Method: Chop the onions & tomato separately using a string chopper or using a knife according to your convenience. Finely chop the ginger, garlic & green chilli as well. Set a kadhai over high flame, add ghee & oil, allow it to heat up as well. Then, add all the whole spices & let them crackle for a few seconds & then add the chopped onions, stir well & cook over high flame until the onions turn golden brown. Once the onions turn light golden brown, add the chopped ginger garlic & green chilli, stir & continue to cook until the onions turn completely golden brown. Then lower the flame & add all the powdered spices along with a splash of hot water, stir well & cook over high flame for 2-3 minutes. Next, add the chopped tomatoes along with whisked curd, cashew paste & salt, stir well & cook over high flame until the gravy turns crumbly & all the ghee gets separated. Once the gravy is cooked, add hot water to adjust the consistency of the gravy, it should be semi-thick and not too thin, then add the sweet corn, stir well, cover & cook over low flame for 4-5 minutes. Then take the lid off & add the diced capsicum, stir well & cook for 2-3 minutes. Taste & adjust salt if required & lastly add kasuri methi powder, garam masala & fresh coriander. Your delicious, quick & easy Corn Capsicum Masala is ready, serve hot with some tandoori rotis or other Indian bread of your choice. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #corncapsicummasala Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Prep 0:44 Cooking Process 1:44 Plating 5:11 Outro 6:11

instructions: Soak the rajma in water for 6-8 hours or overnight, then discard the water & wash them thoroughly to get rid of any smell. | Add the soaked rajma into a pressure cooker along with the whole spices, salt, water & pressure cook over high flame for one whistle then over medium flame for 2-3 more whistles. | Switch off the flame & let the cooker depressurize naturally, then open the lid & check if the rajma is cooked, if not then pressure cook them for 1 more whistle. | Discard the whole spices & keep the cooked rajma aside. | Add the onions into a mixer jar & grind them into a paste. | Similarly, add all the ingredients of the tomato puree & blend them into a fine puree. | Set a kadhai over high heat, add ghee & oil, let it get hot. | Further add cumin seeds & onion paste, stir well & cook over high flame until it turns golden brown, you can add splashes of hot water if the paste starts sticking to kadhai. | Lower the flame & add all the powdered spices with a splash of water, stir well & cook the spices over high flame for a minute or two. | Next, add the tomato puree with salt & cook until the ghee separates & the masala becomes crumbly, this will take 10-12 minutes, if it becomes dry then add hot water & cook it further. | Then add the cooked rajma, stir well & simmer it for 6-8 minutes or until it becomes semi-thick. | Mash a few rajma to thicken up the gravy faster. | Once the gravy becomes semi-thick, dry roast kasuri methi over medium flame in a separate pan then transfer it into a small bowl, crush it between your hands & add it to the gravy. | Then in the same pan heat some ghee & add it to the rajma, along with yellow chilli powder, ginger, green chilli, garam masala & fresh coriander, stir well. | Your delicious, dhaba style rajma masala is ready, serve it with some hot tandoori rotis or bread kulcha with jeera rice.

CINNAMON | दालचीनी 2 INCH BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलाइची 1 NO. CLOVES | लौंग 3 NOS. WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED (1 INCH ABOVE THE RAJMA) SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH Onion Paste ONION | प्याज 3 MEDIUM SIZED. (ROUGHLY CUT) Tomato Puree TOMATO | टमाटर 3 MEDIUM SIZED. (ROUGHLY CUT) GARLIC | लहसुन 15 CLOVES GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH GREEN CHILLI | हरी इलाइची 2 NOS. Tempering and Final Cooking GHEE | घी 2 TBSP OIL | तेल 1 TBSP CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/4 TSP ONION PASTE | प्याज़ कि पेस्ट HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 2 TBSP CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर 1 TSP DRY MANGO POWDER | अमचूर पाउडर 1 TSP BLACK SALT | काला नमक A LARGE PINCH HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED TOMATO PUREE | टमाटर कि प्युरी SALT | नमक TO TASTE KASURI METHI | कसूरी मेथी 1 TBSP GHEE | घी 1-2 TBSP (OPTIONAL) YELLOW CHILLI POWDER | पीली मिर्च का पाउडर 1 TSP GINGER JULIENNE | अदरक के लच्छे A HANDFUL GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (SLICED) FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH Method: Soak the rajma in water for 6-8 hours or overnight, then discard the water & wash them thoroughly to get rid of any smell. Add the soaked rajma into a pressure cooker along with the whole spices, salt, water & pressure cook over high flame for one whistle then over medium flame for 2-3 more whistles. Switch off the flame & let the cooker depressurize naturally, then open the lid & check if the rajma is cooked, if not then pressure cook them for 1 more whistle. Discard the whole spices & keep the cooked rajma aside. Add the onions into a mixer jar & grind them into a paste. Similarly, add all the ingredients of the tomato puree & blend them into a fine puree. Set a kadhai over high heat, add ghee & oil, let it get hot. Further add cumin seeds & onion paste, stir well & cook over high flame until it turns golden brown, you can add splashes of hot water if the paste starts sticking to kadhai. Lower the flame & add all the powdered spices with a splash of water, stir well & cook the spices over high flame for a minute or two. Next, add the tomato puree with salt & cook until the ghee separates & the masala becomes crumbly, this will take 10-12 minutes, if it becomes dry then add hot water & cook it further. Then add the cooked rajma, stir well & simmer it for 6-8 minutes or until it becomes semi-thick. Mash a few rajma to thicken up the gravy faster. Once the gravy becomes semi-thick, dry roast kasuri methi over medium flame in a separate pan then transfer it into a small bowl, crush it between your hands & add it to the gravy. Then in the same pan heat some ghee & add it to the rajma, along with yellow chilli powder, ginger, green chilli, garam masala & fresh coriander, stir well. Your delicious, dhaba style rajma masala is ready, serve it with some hot tandoori rotis or bread kulcha with jeera rice. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #rajmamasalarecipe Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Soaking Rajma 0:48 Cooking Rajma 1:20 Onion Paste 2:07 Tomato Paste 2:18 Rajma Masala 3:35 Plating 7:34 Outro 8:26

title: Tomato Bharta

instructions: Set a papad jali over medium high flame, place the tomatoes & brush some mustard oil on them, roast the tomatoes over direct flame until they get fully charred & soft. | Then place them in a bowl, cover & let them rest for a couple of minutes. | Meanwhile, set a pan over high flame & let it get hot, then add the green chillies, garlic & coriander stems, stir & dry roast all the ingredients for 4-5 minutes until they get charred slightly, then transfer them into a bowl & let them cool down. | Then cut the tops of the green chii& add all the ingredients into a small mixer jar, add very little water & grind it into a thick, coarse paste. | Then peel the tomatoes & chop them up roughly, do not mash them completely, just mince them using a knife, keep them coarse. | For the tempering, set a kadhai over high flame & add mustard oil, heat the mustard oil until it starts smoking, then switch off or lower the flame & let it cool down slightly. | Then add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, curry leaves, prepared paste & onions, stir well & cool over high flame until the onions turn soft. | Then lower the flame & add turmeric powder & kashmiri red chilli powder, stir immediately & then add the tomatoes along with salt, black salt, sugar, stir well & cook for 2-3 minutes. | Lastly taste & adjust salt if required & add garam masala & fresh coriander. | Your delicious, quick & super easy Tomato Bharta is ready. | Serve it hot with some bhakri or roti and rice.

ONION | प्याज़ 2 LARGE (CHOPPED) TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP SALT | नमक TO TASTE BLACK SALT | काला नमक A PINCH SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) Method: Set a papad jali over medium high flame, place the tomatoes & brush some mustard oil on them, roast the tomatoes over direct flame until they get fully charred & soft. Then place them in a bowl, cover & let them rest for a couple of minutes. Meanwhile, set a pan over high flame & let it get hot, then add the green chillies, garlic & coriander stems, stir & dry roast all the ingredients for 4-5 minutes until they get charred slightly, then transfer them into a bowl & let them cool down. Then cut the tops of the green chii& add all the ingredients into a small mixer jar, add very little water & grind it into a thick, coarse paste. Then peel the tomatoes & chop them up roughly, do not mash them completely, just mince them using a knife, keep them coarse. For the tempering, set a kadhai over high flame & add mustard oil, heat the mustard oil until it starts smoking, then switch off or lower the flame & let it cool down slightly. Then add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, curry leaves, prepared paste & onions, stir well & cool over high flame until the onions turn soft. Then lower the flame & add turmeric powder & kashmiri red chilli powder, stir immediately & then add the tomatoes along with salt, black salt, sugar, stir well & cook for 2-3 minutes. Lastly taste & adjust salt if required & add garam masala & fresh coriander. Your delicious, quick & super easy Tomato Bharta is ready. MELON SEEDS | खरबूजे के बीज 1 TBSP PROCESSED CHEESE | प्रोसेस्ड चीज़ 2 CUBES / 50 GRAMS GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. GARLIC | लहसुन 10-12 CLOVES GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH FRESH CREAM | फ्रेश क्रीम 2 TBSP WATER | पानी 1 TBSP HUNG CURD | टंगा हुआ दही 200-250 GRAMS CORN FLOUR | कॉर्न फ्लोर 2 TBSP SALT | नमक TO TASTE BLACK PEPPER POWDER | काली मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP (FRESHLY CRUSHED) POWDERED SUGAR | पीसी हुई शक्कर 1 TBSP CARDAMOM POWDER | इलायची पाउडर 1/4 TSP ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी कसूरी मेथी पाउडर 1/4 TSP BLACK SALT | काला नमक A PINCH CHAAT MASALA | चाट मसाला A PINCH BUTTER | मक्खन 1 TBSP (SOFT) For Smoky Flavour LIVE CHARCOAL | जलता हुआ कोयला GHEE | घी Cooking GHEE | घी 1 TSP MELTED BUTTER | पिघला हुआ मक्खन AS REQUIRED Method: Cut the broccoli head into large size florets, you'll need about 300 grams of broccoli florets. Submerge the florets into boiling water, add a large pinch of salt, stir & let them cool for 4-5 minutes or until fork tender. Do not boil then until they turn extremely soft, the broccoli should remain firm. Transfer the broccoli into ice cold water & let them cool down for a few minutes, then strain them into a sieve. Make sure that the broccoli is completely dry, you can also pay dry them using a clean kitchen napkin. To make the marinade boil the cashews & meon seeds for a few minutes, then strain & transfer them into a bowl & allow them to cool down completely. Transfer the cashews & melon seeds into a mixer jar along with, cheese, green chilli, garlic, ginger, fresh cream & very little & grind it into a smooth, fine paste. Add the hung curd into a large bowl along with the prepared paste & add corn flour along with the powdered spices & butter, whisk well to make a smooth lump free marinade. To give the marinade a smoky flavour, place a small bowl with live charcoal inside it, add a spoonful of ghee, cover & let the marinade smoke for 4-5 minutes, then uncover & discard the charcoal carefully. Coat the blanched broccoli florets with the marinade & skewer them, about 3-4 florets on one skewer should be enough. Coat & skewer all the broccoli florets. To cook them on a grill pan, heat the pan over high flame & then add very little ghee. Once the ghee heats up, place the skewers 3-4 at a time if you have a large enough pan, cook the tikkas over medium high and until golden brown from one side then turn & cook them similarly on the other side. Once cooked, you can apply some melted butter & hold the skewers over direct flame to replicate that charred effect of tandoor. The second method is cooking the tikka over direct flame, just hold the skewer over direct flame & rotate it at regular intervals until the marinade get cooked, it will turn brown from some spots & get charred beautifully over direct flame, you can apply some melted butter & continue cooking it over flame. This method works best if you want to replicate the perfect texture similar to a tandoor, however it is a bit time consuming. To cook it in an air-fryer or anything oven, preheat the airfyer or oven at 200°C, place the skewers onto the tray & cook them for 10-12 minutes. After 5-6 minutes, apply some butter on the tikkas & continue to cook them. Your delicious Broccoli Malai Tikka is ready. raw_text: Buy now - BLP ✨ Amazon - https://amzn.in/d/hKn235A Website - https://yflhome.com/products/blendlabpro2 Zepto - https://www.zeptonow.com/DeepLink?id=6e258a8d-0191-4ca0-bb23-c9fa65d48c86 Full written Recipe -- Chicken Ghee Roast Prep time: 10-15 minutes (1-2 hours soaking time for chillies) Cooking time: 40-45 minutes Serves: 5-6 people Ingredients: Marination CHICKEN | चिकन 1 KG (CURRY CUT) SALT | नमक TO TASTE TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP CURD | दही 2 TBSP Ghee Roast Paste BYADGI RED CHILLI | ब्यादगी लाल मिर्च 25-30 NOS. GUNTUR RED CHILLI | गुंटूर लाल मिर्च 5 NOS. CASHEW | काजू 8 NOS. CORIANDER SEEDS | साबुत धनिया 2 TBSP CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च 1 TSP POPPY SEEDS | खस खस 1 TSP FENUGREEK SEEDS | मेथी के दाने A PINCH GARLIC | लहसुन 20 CLOVES TAMARIND | इमली SMALL LEMON SIZED BALL JAGGERY | गुड़ 1 TBSP WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED (for grinding) Cooking Chicken GHEE | घी 1-2 TBSP Final Cooking GHEE | घी 4-5 TBSP (added in batches) SALT | नमक TO TASTE CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता A SMALL HANDFUL Method: Add washed & clean chicken into a large bowl, add all the ingredients of the marinade, mix well & let the chicken marinate in the fridge while you do the further cooking. Deseed the byadgi red chillies by cutting the top & then cutting splitting them down the middle, add them into a bowl along with the Guntur chillies, cashews & submerge with hot water, cover & let them soak for 1-2 hours. Once all the ingredients are soaked, set a pan over high flame, let it get hot then lower the flame & add coriander seeds, cumin seeds & black peppercorns, stir well & dry roast then while stirring continuously until fragrant. Once they are roasted, add the poppy seeds, stir well & roast only for a few seconds then transfer the spices into a bowl & add the fenugreek seeds, let the spices cool down completely. Add the roasted spices into a mixer jar along with soaked chillies & cashews, garlic, tamarind, jaggery & very little, grind all the ingredients into a thick, fine paste. To cook the chicken, set a kadhai over high heat, add ghee & let it get hot. Then add the marinated chicken, stir & cook over high flame until the chicken is almost cooked (about 90%). Keep stirring the chicken, it will release some of its moisture, cook until the water gets evaporated & only ghee is left. Further, switch off the flame, cover the chicken & set it aside until you cook the paste. Set a tawa or any flat surfaced vessel over high flame, let it get hot then add 2-3 tbsp of ghee in the beginning & let the ghee heat up as well. Next, add the prepared paste, stir well & cook over high while stirring continuously. You have to cook the paste until it turns crumbly, almost a jam like consistency & all the ghee gets separated. This process will take 15 minutes in total, depending upon how much water you have added. After about 6-8 minutes, the paste will turn extremely dry, so to cook it further add 1-2 tbsp of ghee, stir well & continue to cook it further. Traditionally ghee is added, you can also add some hot water to cook it further. Once the paste turns into a jam-like texture & all the ghee gets separated, you will see that the colour will also have deepened, at this stage, add the cooked chicken, stir well & cook the chicken in the ghee roast masala for 4-5 minutes. Lastly taste & adjust salt if required & add curry leaves, stir well. Your delicious, Kundapura Style Chicken Ghee Roast is ready, serve hot with some Neer Dosa or any Indian roti/bread of your choice. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #gheeroast #chickengheeroast Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Marinating Chicken 1:23 Soaking Ingredients for paste 1:46 Ghee Roast Paste 2:45 Cooking Chicken 5:23 Cooking Ghee Roast 6:20 Plating 9:10 Outro 10:19

raw_text: Black Bowl - https://links.yflhome.com/yflhomeblackbowlyt Full written recipe - Punjabi Kheer Puda Prep time: 30-40 minutes (includes soaking & resting time) Cooking time: 40-45 minutes Serves: 5-6 people Kheer Ingredients: TUKDA BASMATI | टुकड़ा बासमती 1/4 CUP WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED (for washing & soaking the rice) MILK | दूध 1 LITRE (FULL FAT) CONDENSED MILK | कंडेंस्ड मिल्क 200 ML / 275 GRAMS ALMONDS | बादाम 1/4 CUP (SOAKED, PEELED & SLICED) Method: Wash & soak the rice in a bowl for 15-20 minutes. Then discard the water & add the soaked rice into a pressure cooker. Further add the milk & stir it well. Put the cooker’s lid on & cook over high flame for exactly 5 minutes or until the pressure builds, then place the cooker on the smallest burner & cook it over the lowest flame for 30 minutes. Then switch off the flame & let the cooker de-pressurize naturally. Open the pressure cooker’s lid & stir the kheer, the milk will have reduced & the colour has changed slightly. Switch on the flame & bring the kheer to a boil. Further add the condensed milk & cook the kheer further, if you are going to serve it hot then cook it until the desired consistency is achieved, but if you are going to serve it cold then keep it slightly runny as the kheer will thicken up significantly once cooled. So, if you are going to serve it chilled, do no cook it for more them 6-8 minutes. Lastly add the slivered almonds & your delicious Kheer is ready. Bring it down to room temperature & keep it in the fridge while you make the Puda. Jaggery Pude Ingredients: JAGGERY | गुड़ 1/2 CUP OR 80 GRAMS (CHOPPED) HOT WATER | गरम पानी 1 CUP SAFFRON | केसर A FEW STRANDS WHEAT FLOUR | गेहूं का आटा 1 CUP / 120 GRAMS FENNEL SEEDS | सौंफ 2 TSP (COARSELY CRUSHED) CARDAMOM POWDER | इलायची पाउडर 1/4 TSP BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च 1 TSP (COARSELY CRUSHED) SALT | नमक A PINCH WATER | पानी 3 TBSP OR AS REQUIRED (for adjusting consistency of the batter) GHEE | घी AS REQUIRED (for cooking) Method: Add the hot water to the jaggery & add the saffron strands as well, stir it & let it rest for a couple of minutes until the jaggery melts completely & the water comes down to room temperature. Meanwhile, add the wheat flour into a large bowl along with all the remaining fry ingredient & mix well. Once the jaggery melts, add the jaggery water gradually into the bowl & whisk well to make lump-free smooth batter. Once you have added all the jaggery water & if the batter seems too thick, you can add more water, 1 tablespoon at a time, it should be a semi-thick flowing batter. Cover & let the batter rest for 30 minutes. To cook the puda, I prefer to shallow fry them on a tawa. Set a tawa over high flame & let it get got, then add 1-2 teaspoons of ghee & let it heat up. Further pour a small ladle full of the batter, it will form into a circular shape on its own. I like to make mini pude, you can adjust the size as per your preference by adding more or less batter at a time. If you are making mini pude then you can cook multiple at a time like how I have. Cook the pude over medium high flame until the turn crisp from once side, then flip & cook them similarly on the other side. Your deliciousPudas are ready, serve them hot with some chilled kheer #yfl #SanjyotKeer #kheer #pude #malpua Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Soaking Rice 1:17 Cooking Kheer 1:34 Malpua Batter 6:18 Cooking Malpua 9:36 Plating 10:41 Outro 12:17

(DICED) | CARROT | गाजर 1 SMALL SIZED (DICED) | CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 1/2 MEDIUM SIZED OR 1 SMALL (DICED) | GREEN PEAS | हरे मटर 1/4 CUP | HOT WATER | गर्म पानी 3 CUPS | SALT | नमक TO TASTE | SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH | BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च 1/2 TSP (FRESHLY CRUSHED) | LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF HALF LEMON | FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) | GHEE | घी 1 TSP (OPTIONAL)
instructions: Before making the upma, make sure that you cut the veggies into small dices. | You can mix all the veggies into one bowl as they will be added together, just keep the onion separately. | Set a fry pan over medium heat, once it gets slightly hot add ghee & let it get hot, then add the rawa & roast it over medium to low flame for 3-4 minutes while stirring it continuously. | Once roasted, transfer the rawa into a bowl & set it aside to cool down. | It is very important that you use upma rawa and not fine rawa. | To fry the nuts, set a kadhai over high flame then add the ghee & let it get moderately hot. | Then add the cashews & peanuts, fry them over medium to low flame until they turn light golden brown, then transfer them into a bowl & set it aside. | Now in the same kadhai, add mustard seeds & let them crackle, then add the urad dal & chana dal, stir & cook over low flame until the dals get light golden brown. | Further add the ginger, green chilli & onion, stir well & cook over high flame until the onion turns translucent, then add the curry leaves & stir well. | Then add all the veggies & cook them over a high flame for 2-3 minutes. | Then measure & add the water along with salt, sugar & black pepper, let the water come to a rolling boil. | The perfect ratio of Rawa to Water for the perfect upma is 1:3. | So 1 cup of rawa & 3 cups of water. | Use the same cup measure to measure both. | Then add the roasted rawa while stirring it continuously to avoid lumps, cook over medium high flame while stirring continuously until there is no separate water visible. | Then cover & cook over low flame for 4-5 minutes, open the lid & stir it once after 2 minutes. | Once cooked over low flame, remove the lid & stir the upma, then put the lid back on, switch off the flame & let it rest for 5 minutes, this step is very important. | Next, remove the lid & add the fried nuts, lemon juice, fresh coriander & ghee, stir well. | Your perfect, melt in the mouth Vegetable Upma is ready, serve it hot with some coconut chutney & sambar.

(DICED) CARROT | गाजर 1 SMALL SIZED (DICED) CAPSICUM | शिमला मिर्च 1/2 MEDIUM SIZED OR 1 SMALL (DICED) GREEN PEAS | हरे मटर 1/4 CUP HOT WATER | गर्म पानी 3 CUPS SALT | नमक TO TASTE SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च 1/2 TSP (FRESHLY CRUSHED) LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF HALF LEMON FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) GHEE | घी 1 TSP (OPTIONAL) Method: Before making the upma, make sure that you cut the veggies into small dices. You can mix all the veggies into one bowl as they will be added together, just keep the onion separately. Set a fry pan over medium heat, once it gets slightly hot add ghee & let it get hot, then add the rawa & roast it over medium to low flame for 3-4 minutes while stirring it continuously. Once roasted, transfer the rawa into a bowl & set it aside to cool down. It is very important that you use upma rawa and not fine rawa. To fry the nuts, set a kadhai over high flame then add the ghee & let it get moderately hot. Then add the cashews & peanuts, fry them over medium to low flame until they turn light golden brown, then transfer them into a bowl & set it aside. Now in the same kadhai, add mustard seeds & let them crackle, then add the urad dal & chana dal, stir & cook over low flame until the dals get light golden brown. Further add the ginger, green chilli & onion, stir well & cook over high flame until the onion turns translucent, then add the curry leaves & stir well. Then add all the veggies & cook them over a high flame for 2-3 minutes. Then measure & add the water along with salt, sugar & black pepper, let the water come to a rolling boil. The perfect ratio of Rawa to Water for the perfect upma is 1:3. So 1 cup of rawa & 3 cups of water. Use the same cup measure to measure both. Then add the roasted rawa while stirring it continuously to avoid lumps, cook over medium high flame while stirring continuously until there is no separate water visible. Then cover & cook over low flame for 4-5 minutes, open the lid & stir it once after 2 minutes. Once cooked over low flame, remove the lid & stir the upma, then put the lid back on, switch off the flame & let it rest for 5 minutes, this step is very important. Next, remove the lid & add the fried nuts, lemon juice, fresh coriander & ghee, stir well. Your perfect, melt in the mouth Vegetable Upma is ready, serve it hot with some coconut chutney & sambar. instructions: While making poha, always make sure that you get the thick variety of poha, Jada Poha. | The other variety is thin which is used to make chivda. | Place a large sieve on a bowl, add the or a plate & let it rest for 10-15 minutes or until you prepare the veggies. | Cut all the veggies into small dice & chop the onion. | Keep the onion in a separate bowl, potato in a separate bowl & you can mix all the other veggies in a common bowl or a plate. | Once the poha has rested, gently mix it using your hand to fluff it up, this is the perfect way to soak the poha, you’ll see that they won’t turn out sticky or clumpy. | Set a kadhai over high flame, once it gets hot, add the oil & heat it well. | Further add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, green chill, curry leaves & peanuts, mix well & cook over low flame briefly & then add the potatoes. | Cook the potatoes over high flame until they are half cooked, make sure you half cook the potatoes before adding the onions, if you add potatoes & onion together then the onions will get overcooked. | Once the potatoes are half done, add in the chopped onion, stir well & cook until the onion turns almost light golden brown over high flame. | Next, add all the prepared veggies, stir well & cook over high flame for 1-2 minutes, then add a splash of hot water, cover & cook over low flame for 3-4 minutes until the carrots & the beans get cooked. | Once the veggies get cooked, remove the lid, lower the flame at this stage & add salt & turmeric powder, mix well & add the soaked poha along with lemon juice, sugar & fresh coriander, stir everything very gently, then you can taste & adjust salt & sugar as per your taste preference. | Further add a splash of hot water over the poha & cover with a lid, switch off the flame & let the poha rest for 3-4 minutes, following this step will ensure that the poha turns out to be very moist & fluffy due to the steam. | Remove the lid after 3-4 minutes & sprinkle some freshly chopped coriander & your delicious & healthy vegetable poha is ready. | I like to add some sev & fresh pomegranate while serving.

(CHOPPED) TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP KASHMIRI RED CHILL POWDED | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP SPICY RED CHILL POWDER | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TBSP DRY MANGO POWDER | आमचूर पाउडर 1 TSP HOT WATER | गरम मसाला A SPLASH SALT | नमक TO TASTE HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED SUGAR | शक्कर 1 TSP ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | कसूरी मेथी पाउडर A LARGE PINCH GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) Method: Wash & peel the bottle gourd, then divide it into smaller pieces & grate it using a large hole grater. Try getting a smaller sized bottle gourd, you don't want the seeds to be very large inside otherwise you will need to cut the core out. Add the grated lauki onto a muslin cloth, add a pinch of salt, mix & let it rest for a few minutes. Next, squeeze out the excess moisture from the lauki, make sure you don't make it completely dry, it needs a very little amount of moisture. Before making the kofta mixture, set a kadhai with oil over medium flame, so by the time you shape the kofta the oil will get hot. Add the lauki into a bowl & add all the ingredients of the kofte, mix well & gently mash using your hands, the mixture should be very slightly loose but should still bind into balls. If the mixture is very moist then you can a more besan, and if it becomes to dry then you can add splashes of water. Once the mixture is ready, shape it into medium sized koftas. Once the oil is moderately hot, 150°C, carefully drop the shaped koftas into the oil, let them fry for 2-3 minutes before stirring. Once they hold their structure, stir & fry them slowly over medium low flame until golden brown. If you fry them over high flame the koftas will remain raw from the inside. Once fried, transfer them into a sieve & set them aside until you make the gravy, fry them in batches. Add the tomatoes & cashews into a mixer jar & blend into a fine puree. Set a heavy bottomes vessel over high flame, add ghee & oil, let it get hot. Then add all the whole spices & cook them for a few seconds. Add onions, stir well & cook over medium high flame until they turn light golden brown. Next, add the ginger garlic paste along with green chillies, stir & continue to cook further until the onions turn completely golden brown. Then lower the flame & add all the powdered spices along with a splash of hot water, stir & cook over high flame for 2-3 minutes. Once the oil separates, add the prepared tomato & cashew puree along with salt, stir well, cover & cook the gravy over low flame for 12-15 minutes. Open the lid & stirr the gravy at regular intervals, add hot water & continue to cook further if the gravy gets too dry. Once the gravy is cooked, add hot water to adjust the consistency of the gravy, keep it slightly thin. Taste & adjust salt if required & add sugar, kasuri methi powder & garam masala. Add the prepared kofte along with some coriander & cook the koftas in the gravy briefly. Our delicious, lauki ke kofte as ready, serve them with tandoori roti, chapati or any bread of your choice. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #laukikekofte #koftacurry Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Preparing Kofte 1:19 Gravy & final cooking 6:21 Plating 9:29 Outro 10:22

CORIANDER SEEDS | साबुत धनिया 2 TSP CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च 1/2 TSP CAROM SEEDS | अजवाइन A PINCH (OPTIONAL) FENUGREEK SEEDS | मेथी के बीज A PINCH GARLIC | लहसुन 8-10 CLOVES GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH ONION | प्याज़ 1 NO. TAMARIND | इमली 2 TBSP (LEMON SIZED BALL) FRESH COCONUT | ताज़ा नारियल 1 CUP (SCRAPED) TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED Tempering & final cooking COCONUT OIL | नारियल तेल 2 TBSP MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 10-12 NOS. GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 1 NO. (SLIT) ONION | प्याज़ 1 NO. (CHOPPED) HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED PRAWNS | झींगे 300 GRAMS (CLEANED & DEVIENED) SALT | नमक A PINCH LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस 2 TSP COCONUT MILK | नारियल का दूध 2-3 TBSP Method: Set a pan over high heat & let it get hot, then lower the flame & add the coconut oil, add the red chillies & roast the chillies over low flame while stirring continuously until they get hot & puff up slightly. Transfer the chillies into a bowl & set them aside. In the same pan add all the whole spices of the paste, & roast them over medium flame until they turn fragrant & golden brown in colour. Transfer the spices into the same bowl as the chillies & let all the ingredients cool down completely. Once cooled, transfer the mixture into a mixer grinding jar & add garlic, ginger, onion, tamarind, fresh coconut, turmeric powder, water & grind it into a smooth paste. Do not add a lot of water, keep the paste thick. To make the curry, set a stock pot over high flame, add the coconut oil & let it get hot. Add mustard seeds, let them crackle then add curry leaves, green chilli & onion, stir well & cook over medium high flame until the onions turn soft. Next, add the prepare paste, stir well & cook over high flame for 4-5 minutes or until a slight glaze appears on the paste. Add hot water to make a thin curry, stir well & bring it to a boil then let it simmer for 12-15 minutes over medium flame. The curry will cook nicely & thicken up, which will completely change the flavour profile of the gravy. Meanwhile, as an optional step, you can add a pinch of salt & some lemon juice to the prawns, it will get rid of any off-smell present in the prawns. After simmering the curry for 12-15 minutes, add the prawns, stir it gently & cook for 5-6 more minutes until the prawns get fully cooked. Once the prawns get cooked, you can adjust the consistency of they curry as per your preference, cook it further for a few more minutes if you want the curry to be thick or add hot water if you like the curry to be thin. Taste & adjust salt if required & optionally you can also add some coconut milk. Your super delicious, quick & easy Mangalorean Prawn Curry is ready. title: Eggless Mango Milk Cake Recipe in a Pan | No Oven | बिना ओवन के बनाइये मैंगो केक | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description: Mango Milk Cake (No oven, No Microwave, No Cooker) Prep time: 10-15 minutes Cooking time: 45-50 minutes Serves: 8 slices

instructions: Add the milk to a bowl, then add the vinegar. | Stir gently and let it rest for 2–3 minutes. | This acts as a buttermilk replacement, the vinegar helps make the cake softer. | Next, add the melted butter and vanilla to the milk mixture, and whisk well to make a lump-free wet mixture. | Place a sieve over the bowl and add the refined flour along with all the remaining dry ingredients. baking, prepare the mango-flavoured milk. | Cut the mango and add all the pulp to a mixer jar. | Add a few ladles of milk and blend. | Strain the mixture and combine it with the remaining milk. | Add
