raw_text: Mango Milk Cake (No oven, No Microwave, No Cooker) Prep time: 10-15 minutes Cooking time: 45-50 minutes Serves: 8 slices Ingredients: Vanilla Sponge MILK | दूध 1 CUP / 240 ML (LUKEWARM) VINEGAR | सिरका 2 TSP BUTTER | मख्हन 1/3 CUP / 70 GRAMS VANILLA ESSENCE | वैनिला एसेंस1 TSP REFINED FLOUR | मैदा 1 CUP / 160 GRAMS POWDERED SUGAR | पीसी हुई शक्कर 1/2 CUP / 70 GRAMS BAKING POWDER | बेकिंग पाउडर 1 TSP BAKING SODA | बेकिंग सोडा 1/2 TSP Mango flavoured milk mixtture MILK | दूध 2 CUPS / 480 ML CONDENSED MILK | कंडेंस्ड मिल्क 1/2 CUP / 160 GRAMS FRESH CREAM | फ्रेसज क्रीम 1/2 CUP / 120 GRAMS MANGO | आम 1 NO. Toppings / Garnish WHIPPPED CREAM | व्हिप्पड क्रीम MANGO | आम Method: Add the milk to a bowl, then add the vinegar. Stir gently and let it rest for 2–3 minutes. This acts as a buttermilk replacement, the vinegar helps make the cake softer. Next, add the melted butter and vanilla to the milk mixture, and whisk well to make a lump-free wet mixture. Place a sieve over the bowl and add the refined flour along with all the remaining dry ingredients. Sift everything together. Whisk until a smooth, lump-free batter is formed. Do not over-whisk. Transfer the batter into a non-stick fry pan and tap it a few times gently to release any air bubbles. Place the pan on the stove, cover it with a lid, and cook on the lowest possible flame for 15 minutes. If the lid has a steam vent, seal it with a piece of foil. While the sponge is baking, prepare the mango-flavoured milk. Cut the mango and add all the pulp to a mixer jar. Add a few ladles of milk and blend. Strain the mixture and combine it with the remaining milk. Add condensed milk and fresh cream, stir well & the mango-flavoured milk is ready. After 15 minutes of cooking, remove the lid and check the sponge. The sides and edges will be cooked, but the top may look slightly raw. Switch off the flame, cover the pan again, and let it rest for 10–15 more minutes. The residual heat will finish the cooking. Once rested, open the lid and check doneness by inserting a toothpick, it should come out clean. Let the sponge cool completely before demoulding. Use a spatula to lift it out gently, it should release easily. Carefully flip it onto a large plate or chopping board and cut it into 8 equal slices. Poke holes into the sponge using a fork. Pour a few ladles of the mango milk onto the sponge, ensuring you cover it evenly, including the sides and edges. Reserve the remaining milk to serve later. Cover with a large lid and set it in the fridge for a few minutes to allow the sponge to soak the milk. Meanwhile, whip the cream and chop mangoes for topping. Once cooled and set, serve the delicious mango milk cake. Decorate with whipped cream and chopped mangoes, and pour some of the mango-flavoured milk around each slice while serving. #mango #yfl #SanjyotKeer Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Cake Batter 1:17 Cooking Vanilla Sponge 2:37 Mango Flavoured Milk 5:07 Continuation of Sponge 6:41 Demoulding & Cutting 7:56 Soaking the cake 9:30 10:36 Outro 11:38

title: Meethi Chutney Recipe | Sweet Tamarind Chutney | गुड़ और इमली की खट्टी मीठी चटनी | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description: YFL is celebrating it’s 9th anniversary & we’ve got a surprise for you 😍🥳 Your next order comes with a free gift! 🎁 Pick from a Platter, Dip Bowl, Tote, or Apron. Just our way of adding a little extra joy to your cart! 🤗
ingredients: TAMARIND | इमली 75 GRAMS (DESEEDED) | DATES | खजूर 250 GRAMS (DESEEDED) | HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED | JAGGERY | गुड़ 750 GRAMS (CHOPPED) | GINGER POWDER | सोंठ पाउडर 1 TSP | BLACK PEPPER POWDER | काली मिर्च पाउडर 1/4 TSP | ROASTED CUMIN POWDER | भुना जीरा पाउडर 1 TBSP | BLACK SALT | काला नमक 1 TSP | KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च 1 TBSP | SALT | नमक 1 TSP | WATER | पानी 1 LITRE
instructions: Add seedless dates & seedless tamarind to a bowl. | Pour enough hot water to fully submerge them, cover & let them soak for 30 minutes so they soften up. | Once soaked, transfer them to a mixer grinder & blend into a smooth, lump-free paste, you can add more water if required. | Strain the paste using a sieve into a heavy-bottomed stock pot to remove any fibrous bits from the tamarind, nothing else will be collected in the sieve. | Add all the remaining chutney ingredients to the pot, including jaggery & spices & turn the flame to high. | Stir continuously until the jaggery melts completely. | Once the mixture comes to a boil, reduce the heat to medium & let is simmer. | Remove the scum that will start to float on the surface. | Keep cooking while stirring at regular intervals. | Let it simmer for about 30-40 minutes. | The chutney will get darker & thicker as it cooks & reduces. | After about 30 minutes of cooking, it will get thick enough to coat & glaze the back of the spoon. | You can stop cooking the chutney at this stage if you like it a bit runny but still slightly thick. | It will also thicken up slightly once it cools down. | I like the chutney to be slightly thicken, a proper glazing & coating consistency so I cook it for 10 more minutes, total simmering time of 40 minutes. | Turn off the heat & let the chutney cool completely. | It will continue to thicken as it cools to a perfect chutney consistency that I prefer & is perfect for all types of dishes. | Your meethi chutney is ready! | Store in a sterilized, airtight jar, refrigerate for a few weeks (upto 2 months) or freeze in batches which will last for 6 months to a year.

Pour enough hot water to fully submerge them, cover & let them soak for 30 minutes so they soften up. Once soaked, transfer them to a mixer grinder & blend into a smooth, lump-free paste, you can add more water if required. Strain the paste using a sieve into a heavy-bottomed stock pot to remove any fibrous bits from the tamarind, nothing else will be collected in the sieve. Add all the remaining chutney ingredients to the pot, including jaggery & spices & turn the flame to high. Stir continuously until the jaggery melts completely. Once the mixture comes to a boil, reduce the heat to medium & let is simmer. Remove the scum that will start to float on the surface. Keep cooking while stirring at regular intervals. Let it simmer for about 30-40 minutes. The chutney will get darker & thicker as it cooks & reduces. After about 30 minutes of cooking, it will get thick enough to coat & glaze the back of the spoon. You can stop cooking the chutney at this stage if you like it a bit runny but still slightly thick. It will also thicken up slightly once it cools down. I like the chutney to be slightly thicken, a proper glazing & coating consistency so I cook it for 10 more minutes, total simmering time of 40 minutes. Turn off the heat & let the chutney cool completely. It will continue to thicken as it cools to a perfect chutney consistency that I prefer & is perfect for all types of dishes. Your meethi chutney is ready! Store in a sterilized, airtight jar, refrigerate for a few weeks (upto 2 months) or freeze in batches which will last for 6 months to a year. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #meethichutney Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Soaking Tamarind & Dates 1:35 Making Tamarind & Date Paste 2:35 Special announcement 3:17 Cooking Process 4:46 Storage Tips 10:59 Plating 12:51 Outro 13:17

title: Gujarati Dal (Khatti Meethi Dal)
ingredients: TOOR DAL | तूर दाल 1 CUP | TAMARIND | इमली 2 TBSP | HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED | SALT | नमक TO TASTE | TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP | WATER | पानी 3 CUPS | PEANUTS | मूंगफली 2 TBSP | GHEE | घी 2 TBSP | MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP | CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP | CINNAMON STICK | दालचीनी 1/2 INCH | CLOVES | लौंग 3-4 NOS. | RED CHILLI | लाल मिर्च 2 NOS. | FENUGREEK SEEDS | मेथी के बीज A PINCH | ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/2 TSP | CURRY LEAVES 15 NOS. | GINGER CHILLI PASTE | अदरक मिर्च की पेस्ट 1 TBSP | TOMATO | टमाटर 1 MEDIUM SIZED. (CHOPPED) | SALT | नमक A TO TASTE | TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP | KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/4 TSP | HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED | TAMARIND PULP | इमली का पल्प 4 TBSP | JAGGERY | गुड़ 2-3 TBSP | FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
instructions: Wash the toor dal 2-3 times until the added water turns clear & soak for 30 minutes to an hour. | Add a large lemon sized ball of tamarind into a bowl, add hot water & let it soak until you do the further processes. | Once soaked, discard the water & add the dal into a pressure cooker & add water, turmeric powder & salt. | Place a steel bowl over the dal in the cooker & add the peanuts into the bowl, put the cooker’s lid on & cook the dal over high flame for 4-5 whistles, then switch off the flame & let the cooker de-pressurize. | Once de-pressurized open the lid, remove the peanuts & set them aside, then blend the dal using a whisk or an emulsion blender, the dal should become smooth. | Mash the soaked tamarind to remove all of its pulp & then strain it using a sieve to get tamarind pulp. | To prepare the tempering, set a deep pan or a stock pot over high flame & once it gets hot add in the ghee & let the ghee heat as well. | Once the ghee gets hot add the cumin watery at this stage as it needs to simmer for a while, it will thicken up gradually while cooking. | Add tamarind pulp, jaggery & the peanuts, let the dal come to a boil, then taste & add salt according to your preference, you can also add more tamarind pulp & jaggery as per your preference. | Gujarati dal is not at all about only sweetness, it needs to have the right balance of jaggery & tamarind. | Cook until the dal reaches the right consistency, it should not be too thick nor too thin & lastly add fresh coriander. | Your super delicious & comforting Gujarati Dal is ready. | For the best meal experience, have it with hot rice & serve some salad, papad, pickle & chaas on the side, do not forget to add some more ghee on the top.
raw_text: Full written recipe - Gujarati Dal (Khatti Meethi Dal) Prep time: 10-15 minutes Cooking time: 20-25 minutes Serves: 3-4 people Ingredients: Boiled Toor Dal TOOR DAL | तूर दाल 1 CUP TAMARIND | इमली 2 TBSP HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED SALT | नमक TO TASTE TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP WATER | पानी 3 CUPS PEANUTS | मूंगफली 2 TBSP Tempering & Final Cooking GHEE | घी 2 TBSP MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP CINNAMON STICK | दालचीनी 1/2 INCH CLOVES | लौंग 3-4 NOS. RED CHILLI | लाल मिर्च 2 NOS. FENUGREEK SEEDS | मेथी के बीज A PINCH ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/2 TSP CURRY LEAVES 15 NOS. GINGER CHILLI PASTE | अदरक मिर्च की पेस्ट 1 TBSP TOMATO | टमाटर 1 MEDIUM SIZED. (CHOPPED) SALT | नमक A TO TASTE TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/4 TSP HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED TAMARIND PULP | इमली का पल्प 4 TBSP JAGGERY | गुड़ 2-3 TBSP FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) Method: Wash the toor dal 2-3 times until the added water turns clear & soak for 30 minutes to an hour. Add a large lemon sized ball of tamarind into a bowl, add hot water & let it soak until you do the further processes. Once soaked, discard the water & add the dal into a pressure cooker & add water, turmeric powder & salt. Place a steel bowl over the dal in the cooker & add the peanuts into the bowl, put the cooker’s lid on & cook the dal over high flame for 4-5 whistles, then switch off the flame & let the cooker de-pressurize. Once de-pressurized open the lid, remove the peanuts & set them aside, then blend the dal using a whisk or an emulsion blender, the dal should become smooth. Mash the soaked tamarind to remove all of its pulp & then strain it using a sieve to get tamarind pulp. To prepare the tempering, set a deep pan or a stock pot over high flame & once it gets hot add in the ghee & let the ghee heat as well. Once the ghee gets hot add the cumin seeds, mustard seeds, cloves, cinnamon, red chillies, fenugreek seeds, asafoetida, curry leaves, ginger chilli paste, stir & cook over high flame briefly. Next, add the tomatoes with salt & cook over high flame until the tomatoes turn mushy. Add turmeric powder & kashmiri red chilli powder, stir well & add the cooked toor dal, add hot water to adjust the consistency, keep the dal a little watery at this stage as it needs to simmer for a while, it will thicken up gradually while cooking. Add tamarind pulp, jaggery & the peanuts, let the dal come to a boil, then taste & add salt according to your preference, you can also add more tamarind pulp & jaggery as per your preference. Gujarati dal is not at all about only sweetness, it needs to have the right balance of jaggery & tamarind. Cook until the dal reaches the right consistency, it should not be too thick nor too thin & lastly add fresh coriander. Your super delicious & comforting Gujarati Dal is ready. For the best meal experience, have it with hot rice & serve some salad, papad, pickle & chaas on the side, do not forget to add some more ghee on the top. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #gujaratidal Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Soaking Toor Dal 1:34 Soaking Tamarind 1:58 Cooking Dal 2:10 Tamarind Pulp 4:39 Tempering & Final Cooking 4:55 Plating 8:00 Outro 9:34

DRY ROSE PETALS | गुलाब की पंखुड़ियां 1 TBSP SAFFRON | केसर A PINCH WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED ROCK SUGAR | मिश्री 4 TBSP GINGER POWDER | सोंठ पाउडर 1/2 TSP NUTMEG | जयफल A PINCH (GRATED) CHILLED MILK | ठंडा दूध 1 LITRE (ADDED GRADUALLY) RABDI | रबड़ी 150 GRAMS FRESH CURD | ताज़ा दही 100 GRAMS Method: Add almonds, pistachio, melon, poppy & fennel seeds, black peppercorns, green cardamom, dry rose petals & saffron into a bowl & add water to submerge all the ingredients completely, cover & let them soak for 5-6 hours or overnight. Once soaked, add the mixture into a mixer jar along with the water, then add rock sugar, ginger powder, nutmeg & 300-350 ml of chilled milk, blend it into a fine mixture. Do not blend for a very long period of time, you need the mixture to remain cold. Place a sieve over a large stock pot, keep a muslin cloth over it & add the blended mixture. Stir & let the milk drain out through the cloth & sieve, then bring the corners together, twist & squeeze out all the remaining milk. The thandai concentrate will be collected in the stock pot. Collect & add the leftovers of the paste back into the mixer jar, add 300-350ml of more chilled milk & blend it again & repeat the straining process, this will help to extract all the flavours from the nuts, seeds & spices. Add this stage your basic thandai is ready, you can taste, adjust sweetness if required, chill & serve it but, this recipe also includes the addition of rabdi & fresh curd to enhance the texture & richness of the rabdi. In a bowl add the rabdi, fresh curd & remaining milk & whisk it well, then just using the sieve, add the mixture into the thandai, this will only take a few seconds & mix well. You delicious, rich & creamy thandai is ready, do not add ice to chill it, instead just place it in the fridge for sometime & then serve, you can also optionally garnish it with some saffron strands & slivers nuts. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #thandairecipe Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Soaking 1:35 Information about Thandai 2:01 Blending & Straining 2:33 How to customize your BlendLab Pro 2.0 5:28 Final Process 6:16 Final Tips 6:54 Plating7:39 Outro 8:10

ingredients: ONION | प्याज़ 3 MEDIUM SIZED. | TOMATO | टमाटर 1 MEDIUM SIZED. | GARLIC | लहसुन 20 CLOVES | GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 4-5 NOS. | GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH | FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A SMALL HANDFUL | WATER | पानी VERY LITTLE | PANEER | पनीर 500 GRAMS | OIL | तेल 2-3 TBSP (FOR SHALLOW FRYING) | OIL | तेल 2-3 TBSP | CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A LARGE PINCH | GARLIC | लहसुन 20 CLOVES | TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP | KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP | GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/4 TSP | AGRI MASALA | आगरी मसाला 1 TBSP | SALT | नमक TO TASTE | KOKUM AGAL | कोकम अगल 1 TBSP | HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED | GHEE | घी 2 TBSP (MELTED) | FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
instructions: Chop the onions & tomatoes separately using a chopper or a knife. | For the green paste, add the ginger, garlic, green chilli, fresh coriander & water into a mixer jar & grind it into a fine paste. | Cut the paneer into medium sized rectangular pieces & shallow fry them in a pan until light golden brown from two sides, shallow frying will ensure that the paneer pieces hold their shape during the final cooking process of the recipe. | Set a tawa over high heat, add the oil & let it get hot. | Add the cumin seeds & onions, stir well & cook over medium high flame until the onions turn golden brown. | Once the onions turn light golden brown, add the green paste, garlic cloves & continue to cook over medium high flame until the onions turn golden brown while stirring. | If the masala is getting too dry then you can add some hot water & continue to cook it further. | Lower the flame & add all the powdered spices, a splash of hot water, stir well & cook over high flame for 1-2 minutes or until the oil separates. | Agri masala suits this recipe the best but if you don’t have it, you can substitute it by using ½ a tablespoon of garam masala & ½ to 1 tbsp of spicy red chilli powder depending on your spice preference. | Further add tomato & salt, stir & cook until the oil separates again, you can add splashes of hot water while cooking if the masala gets too dry. | Next, add kokum agal & cook for 2-3 minutes, you can also use tamarind water as a substitute but try using kokum for the best results. | Once the masala is cooked, add some hot water to adjust the consistency & make a semi thin gravy at this stage so that it can coat the paneer pieces. | Place the fried paneer pieces in the tawa, spread evenly & flip the pieces & coat them with the masala. | Add more hot water to adjust the consistency if required then continue to cook for a few minutes until the masala coats the paneer nicely & becomes thick. | Lastly add melted ghee & fresh coriander, your delicious paneer tawa masala is ready. | Serve hot with some pav or bhakri or even rice.

instructions: Dry roast the peanuts in a hot pan until the turn golden brown, then transfer them into a bowl & let them cool down completely. | Chop the peanuts using a knife or a chopped into a coarse powder, do not make a fine powder. | Add the amaranth flour into a large bowl along with add the remaining ingredients of the dough, except for wter then mix all the ingredient using your hands. | Further, gradually add very little water & combine all the dry flour, you need the dough to be semi-stiff, it should not be too soft or it will b very challenging to roll it out into a paratha. | There is no gluten in amaranth flour so there is no need to knead it a lot. | Cover the dough with a slightly damp cloth & let it rest for 10 minutes. | Meanwhile, add all the ingredients of they chutney into a mixer jar & grind it into a fine chutney. | Before you start rolling the paratha, set a tawa over medium high heat & let it get hot. | Take a small portion of the dough & form it into a roundel then slight press it to flatten it. | Coat it with some dry amaranth flour & then gently roll it out into a paratha. | Once the tawa gets hot, gently lift & place the paratha on the tawa & let it cook from one side over medium high flame, do not add ghee at this stage, cook it dry. | Flip the paratha & cook it similarly on the other side until light golden-brown spots start to appear. | Then add ghee & cook until both the sides turn golden brown. | Your delicious Rajgira Paratha is ready, serve it with the special green chutney & sweet curd.

GARLIC | लहसुन 15 CLOVES GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया 2 TBSP WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED Marination MUTTON | मटन 1 KG GREEN PASTE | हरी पेस्ट 2 TBSP SALT | नमक TO TASTE TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP Cooking Mutton OIL | तेल 2 TBSP ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZED. (CHOPPED) HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED Making Kharda (paste) DRY COCONUT | सुखा नारियल 1/2 CUP (GRATED) GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 20 NOS. GARLIC | लहसुन 30 CLOVES GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH OIL | तेल 1 TSP CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A PINCH FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A LARGE HANDFUL FRESH COCONUT | ताज़ा नारियल 1/4 CUP (GRATED) MINT | पुदीना 8-10 LEAVES WATER | प्याज़ AS REQUIRED Final Cooking GHEE | घी AS REQUIRED OIL | तेल 1 TBSP ONION | प्याज़ 1 MEDIUM SIZE. (CHOPPED) GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) Method: To make the green paste add the green chilli, garlic, ginger & fresh coriander into a mixer jar & grind it into a fine paste, some portion of this paste will be used in the marinade & the remaining will be added directly into the kharda in the end. Add the washed & clean mutton into a large bowl, add the green paste, salt & turmeric powder & mix well. Now to cook the mutton, set a pressure cooker over high flame, add oil & let it get hot. Add onion, stir well & cook over high flame until the onions turn slightly golden brown. Next, add the mutton & stir, cook over high flame for 4-5 minutes until you start seeing some colour on the mutton. Further add hot water, the water level should be just below half of the mutton, we do not need a lot of stock for this recipe but if you like having mutton soup then you can add more hot water. Put the lid on & pressure cook the mutton over medium high flame for 5-6 whistles, we only have to cook the mutton until it's 80-90% done. Once cooked, switch off the flame & let the cooker depressurize naturally, then open the lid & check the mutton, it should be slightly under done, if you cook it fully at this stage then when you add it to the kharda it will just disintegrate. Strain the mutton pieces & add them into a separate bowl & remove the stock into a separate bowl, cover & set aside until you make the kharda. Set a pan over high flame & heat it up. Once hot, lower the flame & add the dry coconut & dry roast it while stirring continuously until it turns golden brown, then transfer it into a bowl. In the same pan add the garlic, green chillies & ginger, & cook over high flame while stirring until some light golden brown spots start to appear on the chillies. Then add the oil & cumin seeds, stir well & cook for a few more seconds then transfer this mixture into the same bowl & let all the ingredients cool down completely. Next, add the ingredients into a mixer jar along with fresh coriander, fresh coconut, mint leaves, some water & grind it into a paste. For the final cooking, set a kadhai or a flat bottomed vessel over high flame, add ghee & oil, let it get hot. Add the onions, stir well & cook until it turns slightly light golden brown. Next, add the mutton & cook for 1-2 minutes over high flame while stirring continuously. Then add the prepared kharda, stir well & add 3-4 ladles (600-700ml) of the stock & cook over high flame for 8-10 minutes, the masala will get cooked & it will reach a beautiful coating consistency. During this cooking process the mutton will also get fully cooked. Once the masala has reached a coating consistency & the mutton is cooked, taste & adjust salt if required along with garam masala & the remaining green paste that was reserved in the beginning, stir well & lastly add fresh coriander. Your delicious & spicy Mutton Kharda is ready, serve it with your choice of bhakri and rice. title: Cheesy Paneer Bread Roll
ingredients: PANEER | पनीर 250 GRAMS | PROCESSED CHEESE | प्रोसेस्ड चीज़ 50-100 GRAMS | MIXED BELL PEPPERS | मिक्सड बेल पेपर्स 3/4 CUP (CHOPPED) | GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (CHOPPED) | GINGER | अदरक 1 TSP (CHOPPED) | SPRING ONION GREENS | हरे प्याज़ के पत्ते A HANDFUL | FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) | OREGANO | ऑरिगेनो A PINCH | CHILLI FLAKES | चिली फ्लैक्स A PINCH | DRY MANGO POWDER | आमचूर पाउडर A PINCH | SALT | नमक TO TASTE | SANDWICH BREAD SLICES | सैंडविच ब्रेड स्लाइसिस AS REQUIRED (to make breadrolls)
instructions: Crush & add the paneer into a large bowl then grate & add the cheese using a large hole grate. | Add all the ingredients of the filling & mix well using a spoon, the filling is ready. | To shape the bread rolls, take the bread slices & make sure you use the large size, stack them onto a chopping board & trim off the sides, this step is very important because if you keep the sides then the bread roll won't seal properly which will make the bread roll oily. | Now to shape the bread roll, take one slice & dip it into water, make sure it wet on both the sides, then place the slice between your palms & squeeze out the excess moisture, squeeze it enough to flatten the bread completely. | Flatten the bread slice is crucial as it eliminates the porous texture of the bread, it will again soak in oil while frying if it's not flattened completely. | Now take a handful of the filling, gently squeeze it to bring it together, place it in the center of the bread & bring the corners together, then using your palm & finger press it while rotating it to form an oval / egg shaped bread roll. | There should be no cracks, shape all the bread rolls similarly & let them rest for 10 minutes. | Meanwhile, heat the oil in a deep pan/kadhai until it gets moderately hot (175° C). | Once rested, add 3-4 bread rolls into the hot oil carefully & fry over high flame until crisp & golden brown. | Strain then into a sieve & serve hot with ketchup or any dip of your choice.
raw_text: Full written recipe - Cheesy Paneer Bread Roll Prep time: 5-10 minutes Cooking time: 15-20 minutes Serves: 4-5 people Ingredients: Filling PANEER | पनीर 250 GRAMS PROCESSED CHEESE | प्रोसेस्ड चीज़ 50-100 GRAMS MIXED BELL PEPPERS | मिक्सड बेल पेपर्स 3/4 CUP (CHOPPED) GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (CHOPPED) GINGER | अदरक 1 TSP (CHOPPED) SPRING ONION GREENS | हरे प्याज़ के पत्ते A HANDFUL FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) OREGANO | ऑरिगेनो A PINCH CHILLI FLAKES | चिली फ्लैक्स A PINCH DRY MANGO POWDER | आमचूर पाउडर A PINCH SALT | नमक TO TASTE SANDWICH BREAD SLICES | सैंडविच ब्रेड स्लाइसिस AS REQUIRED (to make breadrolls) Method: Crush & add the paneer into a large bowl then grate & add the cheese using a large hole grate. Add all the ingredients of the filling & mix well using a spoon, the filling is ready. To shape the bread rolls, take the bread slices & make sure you use the large size, stack them onto a chopping board & trim off the sides, this step is very important because if you keep the sides then the bread roll won't seal properly which will make the bread roll oily. Now to shape the bread roll, take one slice & dip it into water, make sure it wet on both the sides, then place the slice between your palms & squeeze out the excess moisture, squeeze it enough to flatten the bread completely. Flatten the bread slice is crucial as it eliminates the porous texture of the bread, it will again soak in oil while frying if it's not flattened completely. Now take a handful of the filling, gently squeeze it to bring it together, place it in the center of the bread & bring the corners together, then using your palm & finger press it while rotating it to form an oval / egg shaped bread roll. There should be no cracks, shape all the bread rolls similarly & let them rest for 10 minutes. Meanwhile, heat the oil in a deep pan/kadhai until it gets moderately hot (175° C). Once rested, add 3-4 bread rolls into the hot oil carefully & fry over high flame until crisp & golden brown. Strain then into a sieve & serve hot with ketchup or any dip of your choice. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #breadrollsrecipe Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Chapters - Intro 0:00 Filling 1:32 Tips for filling 2:36 Shaping 3:18 Additional tips & Frying tips 4:59 Frying 8:30 Plating 9:45 Outro 10:28

(ROUGHLY CHOPPED) SALT | नमक A PINCH HOT WATER | पानी A SPLASH KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP BIRYANI MASALA | बिरयानी मसाला 1 TSP HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH PRAWNS | झींगे (MARINATED) FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) MINT | पुदीना A FEW LEAVES (CHOPPED) KOKUM | कोकम 2-3 NOS. WATER | पानी COCONUT MILK | नारियल का दूध SALT | नमक TO TASTE GHEE | घी 2-3 TBSP Method: The perfect ratio of rice, prawns & water for making a prawns pulao is 1:1:1.5, the quantities for this recipe are as mentioned in the ingredients list. If you want to use only water then you can substitute coconut milk with water. Wash the rice until the added water turns clear & let it soak while you are doing the further processes or for at least half an hour. Add all the ingredients of the green paste into a mixer jar & grind it into a fine paste using very little water. Marinate the prawns with salt, turmeric powder, lemon juice & green paste, set it in the fridge until you make the masala. Set a flat-bottomed vessel over high flame & let it get hot, then add the oil & let it heat up as well. Add all the whole spices, stir & let them crackle for a few seconds. Add the onions, stir & cook them over medium high flame until they turn golden brown. Next, add the green paste with a splash of hot water & cook for a minute. Add the tomato with a pinch of salt, stir & cook until the tomato turns soft, if the masala becomes too dry to cook then add in a splash of hot water & continue to cook. Once the tomato turns soft, lower the flame & add red chilli powder, biryani masala, hot water & stir well, cook over high flame until the oil gets separated. Further add the prawns, fresh coriander, mint & kokum, gently stir them in the masala & cook over high flame for 2 minutes. Drain all the water from the soaked rice & add the rice, stir gently & add the water & coconut milk. Taste & adjust salt, bring the water to a simmer over high flame then cover the vessel with a lid, place a heavy weight on the lid, set the flame to low & let it cook for 12 minutes. Then remove the lid & check, the pulao will be cooked perfectly with no liquid remaining, you can check from the sides, if there is stir liquid present then let it cook off over low flame without covering. Do not stir the pulao at this stage, just switch off the flame, put the lid back on & let it rest for at least 20 minutes. Once rested, gently stir the pulao using a spatula & serve hot. instructions: Peel the potatoes, cut the potato in half then in quarters & cut the quarters into halves, you will get bite sized pieces, keep them submerged in water. | Cut the onions, capsicum & tomatoes (save the core & trimmings) into petals & roughly slice the remaining onions for the gravy. | In a deep pan, add the mustard oil & heat it over high flame until it begins to smoke, then switch off the flame & let the oil cool down slightly. | Once it is only moderately hot, strain the water from the potatoes & carefully add them into the oil, fry the potatoes over high flame while stirring at regular intervals until they get fully cooked & turn golden brown, then strain them out & transfer into a bowl & set aside. | Strain the remaining oil as well, this same oil will be used for the further cooking processes. | Add some of the oil back into the pan, heat it up & add all the whole spices, let them crackle for a few seconds & then add in the sliced onions, stir well & cook them over medium high flame until they turn golden brown. | Further, add garlic, ginger, green chilli, coriander stems, tomato trimmings, salt, stir well & then add a splash of hot water, cover & cook over medium to low flame until the tomatoes turn mushy. | Add all the powdered spices into the curd & mix well to make a smooth mixture, add this mixture into the pan once the tomatoes turn mushy, stir well & cook over high flame until the oil separates, keep adding splashes of hot water if the gravy becomes too dry to cook. | Once the oil separates, switch off the flame & let the mixture cool down completely, then add it into a mixer grinder jar along with some water & grind it into a smooth fine gravy. | In a pan or kadhai, add the remaining mustard oil & heat it up. | Add cumin seeds, coriander seeds, ginger, green chillies & the onion, tomato, capsicum petals, toss over high flame for a minute. | Then add the fried potatoes, saute for a couple of seconds over high flame & then add the prepared gravy along with black salt & chaat masala, stir well & cook for 2-3 minutes. | Taste & adjust salt if required & lastly add kasuri methi powder, garam masala, fresh coriander & stir well. | You can adjust the consistency of the dish as per your preference, if you want the gravy to be thick then do not add any hot water otherwise add some hot water. | Dhaba style Aloo Do Pyaza is ready, serve it with some tandoori roti & salad.

Add some of the oil back into the pan, heat it up & add all the whole spices, let them crackle for a few seconds & then add in the sliced onions, stir well & cook them over medium high flame until they turn golden brown. Further, add garlic, ginger, green chilli, coriander stems, tomato trimmings, salt, stir well & then add a splash of hot water, cover & cook over medium to low flame until the tomatoes turn mushy. Add all the powdered spices into the curd & mix well to make a smooth mixture, add this mixture into the pan once the tomatoes turn mushy, stir well & cook over high flame until the oil separates, keep adding splashes of hot water if the gravy becomes too dry to cook. Once the oil separates, switch off the flame & let the mixture cool down completely, then add it into a mixer grinder jar along with some water & grind it into a smooth fine gravy. In a pan or kadhai, add the remaining mustard oil & heat it up. Add cumin seeds, coriander seeds, ginger, green chillies & the onion, tomato, capsicum petals, toss over high flame for a minute. Then add the fried potatoes, saute for a couple of seconds over high flame & then add the prepared gravy along with black salt & chaat masala, stir well & cook for 2-3 minutes. Taste & adjust salt if required & lastly add kasuri methi powder, garam masala, fresh coriander & stir well. You can adjust the consistency of the dish as per your preference, if you want the gravy to be thick then do not add any hot water otherwise add some hot water. Dhaba style Aloo Do Pyaza is ready, serve it with some tandoori roti & salad. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #aloodopyaza Buy now: BlendLab Pro 2.0 - https://links.yflhome.com/blp2.0ytd Amazon - https://links.yflhome.com/blendlabpro2amazon Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Cutting Potatoes & Veggies 1:07 Frying Potatoes 2:53 Base Gravy 4:03 Final Cooking 8:17 Plating 10:02 Outro 10:46

Bring water to a rolling boil in a stock pot, add vinegar & salt along with the cut potatoes. Cook the potatoes for 3-4 minutes, then remove the potatoes from the water using a large spider & transfer them onto a sieve & let them cool down completely. Make sure you don't overcook the potatoes, you only have to parboil them. To make the batter add the refined flour, corn flour, salt, black pepper powder, baking soda & chilled water into a large bowl & whisk continuously to make a semi thick batter, make sure you add the water gradually while whisking continuously. The consistency of the batter should be semi-thick as mentioned, do not make the batter extremely thick or thin. Further dip & coat the blanched potatoes in the batter & fry them in moderately hot oil (165 C) over medium high flame until light golden brown. Once the potatoes turn light golden brown, transfer them into a sieve & let them rest. Fry the potatoes in batches, do not overcrowd the oil. Set the oil on a separate stove over low flame & let it heat up gradually until it reaches a high temperature (180° C), prepare the sauce first & then flash fry the potatoes before adding them into the sauce for that extra crispiness. To make the sauce, take a wok, add the gochujang and the remaining ingredients of the sauce, mix well & now switch on the flame & bring the sauce to a boil, once the sauce comes to a simmer, cook it briefly until it thickens up. Then switch off the flame & flash fry the potatoes in hot oil for 30 seconds to a minute & add them into the sauce. Toss it well to coat the sauce evenly, do not cook at this stage. Your delicious Korean Crispy Fried Potatoes are ready, serve hot & garnish them with white sesame seeds & spring onion greens. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #koreanpotatoes Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Cutting Potatoes 1:26 Blanching 2:06 Batter 3:35 First Frying 4:34 Ingredient Explanation 5:49 Sauce 7:26 Flash Frying 8:21 Final Tossing 8:39 Plating 8:58 Outro 9:47

title: Atte ki Pinni
ingredients: EDIBLE GUM | गोंद 200 GRAMS | ALMOND | बादाम 150 GRAMS | CASHEW | काजू 150 GRAMS | GHEE | घी 400 GRAMS | WHEAT FLOUR | गेहूं का आटा 500 GRAMS | RAISIN | किशमिश 100 GRAMS | MELON SEEDS | मैगज़ 75 GRAMS | GINGER POWDER | सोंठ 75 GRAMS | CARDAMOM POWDER | इलायची पाउडर 35 GRAMS | POWDERED SUGAR | पीसी हुई शक्कर 400 GRAM | ALMOND | बादाम AS REQUIRED (SPLIT & FRIED)
instructions: Add the edible gum granules into a mixer jar & grind it into a fine powder, set it aside for further use. | Add the nuts into a chopped & grind them coarsely, you can also do the same using a knife. | Set a deep base heavy bottom kadhai on medium heat, add ghee & let it melt, I don’t add all the ghee at once in the beginning as its requirement depends on the weather, so out of the 400 grams I only add 250 grams in the beginning. | Once the ghee melts, add the wheat flour & stir it, combine well with the ghee, the mixture may seem too dry at this stage so add the remaining ghee, the wheat flour should become soft & resemble wet sand. | Keep stirring using the lazy 8 motion & roast the flour over low flame until the colour changes slightly light brown or biscuity. | This is the most important step of making pinni, you have to cook the wheat flour patiently until the right colour is achieved. | The mixture will slowly start to loosen up as you cook it. | As soon as you get the light brown biscuit like colour add the prepared edible gum powder & stir it, the gum will crackle as it gets fried & the texture of the mixture will also change drastically, switch off the flame once all the gum gets mixed. | Add the chopped nuts along with raisins & melon seeds, keep stirring the mixture in the kadhai itself for a few minutes so that the nuts also get cooked slightly, using a wooden spatula you can also mash the mixture, this helps to mix it properly. | Once the temperature drops slightly add the ginger powder, cardamom powder & powdered sugar, stir again while mashing it continuously, then transfer the mixture into a large paraat & mash / beat it using the spatula to help the ghee release from the mixture. | After doing this for a couple of minutes, take a spoonful of the mixture & try to shape a pinni by pressing it in your fist & forming an oval shape laddoo, if the mixture is not binding at this stage, you can add a few spoons of melted ghee & then try to shape it again. | Once the mixture is ready, shape the pinnis, I also like to stick a piece of halved fried almond by pressing it lightly on the pinni, it's completely optional. | This recipe will yield about 40 pieces of pinni, 50 grams each & 2 kgs in total. | Store the pinnis in an airtight container & keep it in a cool dry & hygienic place, enjoy them throughout winters.
raw_text: Full written recipe - Atte ki Pinni Prep time: 5-10 minutes Cooking time: 1 hour 15 minutes Serves: 2 kg (40 medium size pieces) Ingredients: EDIBLE GUM | गोंद 200 GRAMS ALMOND | बादाम 150 GRAMS CASHEW | काजू 150 GRAMS GHEE | घी 400 GRAMS WHEAT FLOUR | गेहूं का आटा 500 GRAMS RAISIN | किशमिश 100 GRAMS MELON SEEDS | मैगज़ 75 GRAMS GINGER POWDER | सोंठ 75 GRAMS CARDAMOM POWDER | इलायची पाउडर 35 GRAMS POWDERED SUGAR | पीसी हुई शक्कर 400 GRAM ALMOND | बादाम AS REQUIRED (SPLIT & FRIED) Method: Add the edible gum granules into a mixer jar & grind it into a fine powder, set it aside for further use. Add the nuts into a chopped & grind them coarsely, you can also do the same using a knife. Set a deep base heavy bottom kadhai on medium heat, add ghee & let it melt, I don’t add all the ghee at once in the beginning as its requirement depends on the weather, so out of the 400 grams I only add 250 grams in the beginning. Once the ghee melts, add the wheat flour & stir it, combine well with the ghee, the mixture may seem too dry at this stage so add the remaining ghee, the wheat flour should become soft & resemble wet sand. Keep stirring using the lazy 8 motion & roast the flour over low flame until the colour changes slightly light brown or biscuity. This is the most important step of making pinni, you have to cook the wheat flour patiently until the right colour is achieved. The mixture will slowly start to loosen up as you cook it. As soon as you get the light brown biscuit like colour add the prepared edible gum powder & stir it, the gum will crackle as it gets fried & the texture of the mixture will also change drastically, switch off the flame once all the gum gets mixed. Add the chopped nuts along with raisins & melon seeds, keep stirring the mixture in the kadhai itself for a few minutes so that the nuts also get cooked slightly, using a wooden spatula you can also mash the mixture, this helps to mix it properly. Once the temperature drops slightly add the ginger powder, cardamom powder & powdered sugar, stir again while mashing it continuously, then transfer the mixture into a large paraat & mash / beat it using the spatula to help the ghee release from the mixture. After doing this for a couple of minutes, take a spoonful of the mixture & try to shape a pinni by pressing it in your fist & forming an oval shape laddoo, if the mixture is not binding at this stage, you can add a few spoons of melted ghee & then try to shape it again. Once the mixture is ready, shape the pinnis, I also like to stick a piece of halved fried almond by pressing it lightly on the pinni, it's completely optional. This recipe will yield about 40 pieces of pinni, 50 grams each & 2 kgs in total. Store the pinnis in an airtight container & keep it in a cool dry & hygienic place, enjoy them throughout winters. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #pinnirecipe Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Grinding edible gum 1:40 Chopping nuts 2:07 Final Cooking Process 3:23 Shaping 7:54 Plating 8:38 Outro (with additional tips) 9:13

title: Gajar Ka Halwa (Pressure Cooker)
ingredients: RED CARROT | लाल गाजर 1/2 KG | GHEE | घी 1 TBSP | MILK | दूध 500 ML (FULL FAT) | SUGAR | शक्कर 1/2 CUP / 100 GRAMS | GREEN CARDAMOM POWDER | इलायची पाउडर A PINCH | GHEE | घी 2 TBSP (for frying nuts) | CASHEW | काजू 2 TBSP (ROUGHLY CHOPPED) | ALMOND | बादाम 2 TBSP (SLICED) | RAISINS | किशमिश 1 TBSP
instructions: Wash the carrots & then trim their tops & bottoms, further peel the carrots & grate them using a large hole grater then transfer them into a bowl. | Set a pressure cooker over high heat & add the ghee, let it get slightly hot & then add all the grated carrot, stir well & cook for 3-4 minutes. | The carrots will release its moisture so cook until the water evaporates. | Further add the milk, stir once then pressure cook over medium flame only for 1 whistle then switch off the flame & let the cooker depressurize naturally. | Next, check the whistle & open the lid, stir well & then switch on the flame to medium high & cook until the milk reduces, this process will take 10-12 minutes, make sure you keep stirring the halwa. | Once almost all the milk has reduced, add the sugar & cardamom powder, stir well & cook it again for 4-5 minutes, the sugar will release its moisture & the halwa will get slightly runny again so cook until it thickens up again. | Do not overcook the halwa or it will become very tight, almost into barfi texture, only until the water from sugar evaporates & the halwa looks a bit shiny then switch off the flame. | Heat a pan, add the ghee into it & let it get slightly hot, then add the almonds & cashews & fry them until they turn light golden brown, keep stirring them. | Once the nuts are almost about to turn light golden brown add the raisins & stir briefly. | Pour the ghee & nuts mixture over the halwa & stir well. | Your gajar ka halwa is ready, let it rest for a couple of minutes, it will soak up all the ghee, heat it up before serving or have as it is, I personally like to have hot halwa with a scoop of vanilla ice cream. | Buy now: https://www.amazon.in/stores/page/5D15146F-D435-48E7-AA01-9E9EE41DB5FC?ref_=cm_sw_r_apin_ast_store_R50PPQ64SBN9C0HGSF6N&store_ref=bl_ast_dp_brandLogo_sto&dplnkId=a847e5ba-694d-47d6-9bf3-fbc7f0677547&dplnk=Y
raw_text: Full Written Recipe - Gajar Ka Halwa (Pressure Cooker) Prep time: 10 minutes Cooking time: 35-40 minutes Serves: 5-6 people Ingredients: RED CARROT | लाल गाजर 1/2 KG GHEE | घी 1 TBSP MILK | दूध 500 ML (FULL FAT) SUGAR | शक्कर 1/2 CUP / 100 GRAMS GREEN CARDAMOM POWDER | इलायची पाउडर A PINCH GHEE | घी 2 TBSP (for frying nuts) CASHEW | काजू 2 TBSP (ROUGHLY CHOPPED) ALMOND | बादाम 2 TBSP (SLICED) RAISINS | किशमिश 1 TBSP Method: Wash the carrots & then trim their tops & bottoms, further peel the carrots & grate them using a large hole grater then transfer them into a bowl. Set a pressure cooker over high heat & add the ghee, let it get slightly hot & then add all the grated carrot, stir well & cook for 3-4 minutes. The carrots will release its moisture so cook until the water evaporates. Further add the milk, stir once then pressure cook over medium flame only for 1 whistle then switch off the flame & let the cooker depressurize naturally. Next, check the whistle & open the lid, stir well & then switch on the flame to medium high & cook until the milk reduces, this process will take 10-12 minutes, make sure you keep stirring the halwa. Once almost all the milk has reduced, add the sugar & cardamom powder, stir well & cook it again for 4-5 minutes, the sugar will release its moisture & the halwa will get slightly runny again so cook until it thickens up again. Do not overcook the halwa or it will become very tight, almost into barfi texture, only until the water from sugar evaporates & the halwa looks a bit shiny then switch off the flame. Heat a pan, add the ghee into it & let it get slightly hot, then add the almonds & cashews & fry them until they turn light golden brown, keep stirring them. Once the nuts are almost about to turn light golden brown add the raisins & stir briefly. Pour the ghee & nuts mixture over the halwa & stir well. Your gajar ka halwa is ready, let it rest for a couple of minutes, it will soak up all the ghee, heat it up before serving or have as it is, I personally like to have hot halwa with a scoop of vanilla ice cream. a pair of tongs, you don’t have to cook a lot at this stage, just mix everything over high flame. | Restaurant style veg schezwan noodles are ready.

raw_text: Written Recipe - Vatidal Na Khaman Prep time: 5-10 minutes Soaking time: 6-8 hours Fermentation time: 8-12 hours Serves: 6-8 people Ingredients: Base Batter CHANA DAL | चना दाल 1 CUP / 215 GRAMS WATER | पानी 1/2 CUP (FOR GRINDING) WATER | पानी 3 TBSP (FOR GRINDING) GRAM FLOUR | बेसन 2 TBSP CITRIC ACID | नींबू का सत 1/4 TSP Final Batter GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 4 NOS. GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 1.5 TBSP ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/4 TSP TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP SUGAR | शक्कर A LARGE PINCH Cooking Khaman SALT | नमक TO TASTE ENO | ईनो 1/2 TSP (for 1 tray) WATER | पानी 1 TSP Tempering GROUNDNUT OIL | मूंगफली का तेल 2-3 TBSP GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 7-8 SLIT (LESS SPICY) MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 2 TSP CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता = 10-12 NOS. WATER | पानी 1/4 CUP SALT | नमक A PINCH FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A LARGE HANDFUL (CHOPPED) Method: Add the chana dal into a bowl & wash it well with water until it turns clear, then add fresh water & let the dal soak for 5-6 hours or overnight. Once soaked, discard the water & add the soaked dal into a mixer grinder jar along with some water & grind it into a thick semi-fine batter, I have given the quantity of the water as per my measurements, just make sure that the batter is not too runny & once grinded the made should feel slightly grainy, just like fine semolina. Transfer the batter into a large bowl or a tall vessel & add the gram flour, citric acid & whisk the batter for 2-3 minutes. Then cover & place it in a warm corner of the kitchen & let it ferment for 8-10 hours or overnight. During winters the batter takes a longer time to ferment so you can wrap the bowl/vessel using a thick cloth. Once fermented, the batter will become airy & almost rise to double in size. Mix the fermented batter gently to release all the excess air & then add the ginger & green chilli paste, groundnut oil, asafoetida, turmeric powder & sugar, whisk well for a minute until all the ingredients get incorporated. The base batter for khaman is ready, if you are planning to make only half of it then you can divide & store the remaining before adding the salt & eno (fruit salt). Before adding the eno, make sure that the steamer is ready & also grease the trays (thalis) with oil in which you are going to steam the khaman. I usually like to steam 1 thali at a time as it allows the khaman to cook more evenly & also prevents the surface of the khaman from getting wet due to condensation. Add salt to the batter & mix well, then transfer half of it into a bowl & add ½ tsp eno along with a tsp of water & mix it thoroughly. Then pour the batter into the greased tray & place it in the steamer, let the khaman steam for 15 minutes & do not open the lid before that as it may affect the rise. After 15 mintues, open the lid & check if the khaman is cooked or not by inserting a knife in the center, it should come out clean. Once cooked, remov the tray from the steamer & let the khaman cool down completely, meanwhile add ½ tsp eno to the remaining batter along with a tsp of water & then steam it similarly. Once cooled, cut the khaman as per your preference & remove them from the tray by scraping with the help of a metal spatula, reserve some smaller & irregular pieces for the chutney. Add all the khaman into a large bowl & prepare the tempering. Set a pan over high flame, add in the oil & let it get hot. Further add the green chillies & fry them briefly, then add the mustard seeds, curry leaves, water & a pinch of salt, make sure that the oil is not extremely hot before adding the water & use a deep pan. Add the tempering over the khaman along with fresh coriander & mix well. Your delicious Vatidal Na Khaman are ready, serve it with some special green chutney & sev. Special Green chutney Ingredients: FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया 1 CUP MINT | पुदीना ½ CUP GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2-3 NOS. GINGER | अदरक ½ INCH KHAMAN | खमण 2-3 PIECES CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP SUGAR | शक्कर 1 TBSP LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF A LEMON SALT | नमक TO TASTE ICE CUBE | बर्फ 2 NOS. WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED Method: Add all the ingredients in a mixer grinder jar & grind it into a fine chutney, add very little water, this chutney needs to be thick in consistency. Your green chutney is ready. title: Perfect Mohanthal Recipe | Danedar Mohanthal | बाज़ार से बेहतर मोहनताल बनाने का तरीका | Sanjyot Keer
ingredients: Grainy gram flour mixture | COARSE GRAM FLOUR | जाड़ा बेसन 2 CUPS / 270 GRAMS | GHEE | घी 2 TBSP / 25 GRAMS | MILK | दूध 2 TBSP / 30 ML | Cooking gram flour | GHEE | घी 3/4 CUP / 150 GRAMS | MILK | दूध 1/2 CUP / 115 ML | SUGAR | शक्कर 1 CUP / 200 GRAMS | WATER | पानी 1/2 CUP / 115 ML | CARDAMOM POWDER | इलाइची पाउडर 1/2 TSP | SAFFRON | केसर A FEW STRANDS | NUTMEG | जायफल A PINCH (GRATED) | ALMONDS | बादाम AS REQUIRED (SLICED) | PISTACHIO | पिस्ता AS REQUIRED (SLICED)
instructions: Add the gram flour into a large bowl along with ghee & milk, mix & rub the gram flour together between your hands then press all the gram flour down onto the bowl together, cover & let raw_text: Full written recipe for Mohanthal Prep time: 5-10 minutes Cooking time: 35-40 minutes (3-4 hours for the mohanthal to set) Serves: 650 grams (approx.) Ingredients: Grainy gram flour mixture COARSE GRAM FLOUR | जाड़ा बेसन 2 CUPS / 270 GRAMS GHEE | घी 2 TBSP / 25 GRAMS MILK | दूध 2 TBSP / 30 ML Cooking gram flour GHEE | घी 3/4 CUP / 150 GRAMS MILK | दूध 1/2 CUP / 115 ML Sugar Syrup SUGAR | शक्कर 1 CUP / 200 GRAMS WATER | पानी 1/2 CUP / 115 ML CARDAMOM POWDER | इलाइची पाउडर 1/2 TSP SAFFRON | केसर A FEW STRANDS NUTMEG | जायफल A PINCH (GRATED) For garnish ALMONDS | बादाम AS REQUIRED (SLICED) PISTACHIO | पिस्ता AS REQUIRED (SLICED) Method: Add the gram flour into a large bowl along with ghee & milk, mix & rub the gram flour together between your hands then press all the gram flour down onto the bowl together, cover & let it rest for 15-20 minutes. Once rested, break the set flour using your hands then sieve it into another large bowl using a large hole sieve (wheat strainer), do not use a fine sieve otherwise you will not be able to achieve the right texture, if any hard lumps of the flour remain, just pulse them using a mixer grinder & then sieve. If you do not have the sieve then you can directly pulse the mixer into tiny grains using the mixer grinder. Before cooking the gram flour, line a tray or a large thali using ghee & butter paper, I used a 9x7 inch aluminum cake tin. Add half quantity of the ghee into a kadhai & let it melt over high flame, then add the prepared gram flour mixture & stir well, once the flour soaks up all the ghee then add the remaining & stir well. Cook the gram flour over low flame while stirring continuously until it turns biscuity in colour, the mixture will loosen up a bit & foam up during the cooking process, this will take about 20-25 minutes. Once it turns biscuity in colour, switch off the flame & add the milk gradually, just a spoonful at a time initially as the mixture will bubble up, so be extremely carefully. Once you have added a few spoons & the mixture has stopped bubbling then you can add all the remaining milk a once, make sure you keep stirring. Switch on the flame to low again & cook the mixture for 4-5 minutes or until a slight glaze appears, the ghee should not separate completely. Then switch off the flame, keep stirring the mixture for 2-3 minutes & let it cool down slightly, if you let it sit in the kadhai then the mixture will darken due to residual heat. In a separate pan, add all the ingredients of the sugar syrup & bring it to a boil over high flame, then lower the flame & cook the syrup once it reaches one-string consistency. To check, take a small amount of syrup on a spoon and let it cool for a few seconds to avoid burns, pinch the syrup between your thumb & index finger, then pull your fingers apart gently, if a single thin string forms between your fingers, the syrup has reached one-string consistency. Switch off the flame & keep stirring the syrup & let it cool down for 2-3 minutes. Add the syrup into the cooked gram flour, stir it for 1-2 minutes until everything gets combined. Then transfer the mohanthal mixture into the tray & spread it evenly then even out the surface using a spatula or a flat bowl. You can optionally add slivered nuts on the mohanthal at this stage & let it set for at least 3 hours of overnight. Once set, remove it using the butter paper & then cut into pieces. Your delicious & melt in the mouth mohanthal is ready. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #Mohantal Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Intro 0:00 Gram Flour Mixture 1:11 Cooking the Gram Flour Mixture 3:47 Sugar Syrup 6:33 Final Process 7:49 Cutting of Mohanthal 9:57 Plating 10:26 Outro 11:10

Once you have acquired the stems, cut the leafy parts from the sides & prep the remaining ingredients the same manner. Further cut the stems into quarters, I like to keep them 2-3 inches long. The inner portion of the stem is edible & the covering is hard & wood like. Wash the cut stems using water, for this recipe you will need about 500 grams of them. Set a pressure cooker over high heat, add ghee & oil, let it get hot. Add cumin seeds along with all the remaining whole spices & onions, stir well & cook the onions over high flame until they turn golden brown. Once the onions turn light golden brown, add the ginger garlic green chilli paste and continue to cook further. Then lower the flame & add all the powdered spices along with a splash of hot water & cook the spices until the ghee separates. title: Kashmiri Laal Paneer Recipe | Waze Tchaman | कश्मीरी लाल पनीर | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description: Written Recipe - Kashmiri Laal Paneer (Waze Tchaman) Prep time: 15-20 minutes (excluding chilli soaking time) Cooking time: 45 minutes – 1 hour Serves: 4-5 people
ingredients: PANEER | पनीर 500 GRAMS | TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1 TSP | MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल AS REQUIRED (FOR SHALLOW FRYING) | FRIED ONION | बरिस्ता 2 MEDIUM SIZED. | WATER | पानी VERY LITTLE | KASHMIRI RED CHILLI | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च 7-8 NOS. (DESEEDED & SOAKED FOR AN HOUR) | WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED | REMAINING MUSTARD OIL | बचा हुआ सरसों का तेल | ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/2 TSP | TOMATO PUREE | टमाटर की प्यूरी 400 GRAMS | KASHMIRI CHILLI PASTE | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च की पेस्ट | TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP | BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलाइची 1 NO. | GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलाइची 3 NOS. | CLOVES | लौंग 3 NOS. | CINNAMON | दालचीनी 2 INCH | FENNEL SEED POWDER | सौंफ का पाउडर 1.5 TBSP | GINGER POWDER | सोंठ पाउडर 2 TSP | SALT | नमक TO TASTE | HOT WATER | गरम पानी 500 ML | FRIED ONION PASTE | बरिस्ता की पेस्ट | ROASTED CUMIN POWDER | भुने जीरे का पाउडर A LARGE PINCH
instructions: Cut the paneer block into thick slices, approximately 2-3 cm thick, you can choose to keep it in a square shape or rectangles, paneer pieces are usually cut & prepared like this in Kashmir. | Bring water to a rolling boil in a stock pot & add turmeric powder, then add the paneer & let it simmer for 5-6 minutes. | Then carefully transfer the paneer pieces onto a plate & let them cool down & dry-off slightly. | Set a pan over high heat, add the mustard oil & let it get hot until it starts smoking then switch off the flamr & let the oil cool down slightly. | Add the paneer pieces & fry them over medium high flame until they turn golden brown from both the sides, then set them aside & reserve the oil to be used further in the recipe. | Add the fried onions into a small mixer jar & grind it into a fine paste using water as required. | Similarly, add the soaked Kashmiri chillies into a mixer jar along with some water & grind it into a fine paste. | Add the reserved mustard oil into a kadhai & let it get hot. | Further add asafoetida, tomato puree, red chilli paste, turmeric powder, all the whole spices, fennel & ginger powder, salt & hot water, stir well & let it come to a boil. | Add the fried onion paste & cook the gravy over medium high flame for 12-15 minutes until the tomato puree gets fully cooked & the gravy reduces. | Then add the fried paneer pieces, stir well, cover & cook over medium low flame for 7-8 minutes. | Once cooked you can adjust the consistency of the gravy as per your preference, authentically it is not too runny. | Lastly just taste & adjust seasoning if required & add roasted cumin powder | Your delicious Kashmiri Laal Paneer is ready, serve it hot with some rice or any bread of your choice.

title: Ajay Devgn Special Mutton Curry Revealed - Ajay Devgn & Aaman Devgan cook with Chef Sanjyot Keer
description: What an amazing time I had cooking with the legendary Ajay Devgn sir and Aaman Devgan! We cooked a delicious mutton curry that I’ve named the Ajay Devgn Mutton Curry as a tribute to his love for food. 💛 It was such a wholesome experience discussing food, favourite dishes, cooking memories, food on film sets, family favourites and the joy of bonding over food. By the way if you guys don't know, Ajay Sir is a true foodie at heart, he loves to cook for his family and he shared some incredible quick food recipes to try at home and Aaman shared exciting insights from the sets of Azaad, as he gears up for his big-screen debut. We all have watched Ajay sir’s films and love his craft but you will be watching him cook for the first time ever on-screen on YFL and I am sure you would love this side of his. Watch this amazing episode and don’t forget to note down the Ajay Devgn Special Mutton Curry recipe and try it at home! 🎬🔥
ingredients: MUTTON | मटन 1 KG | GINGER GARLIC GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक लहसुन हरी मिर्च की पेस्ट 2 TBSP | Salt to taste | Turmeric powder | 1/2 TSP | DRY COCONUT | सुखा नारियल 1/4 CUP (GRATED) | OIL | तेल 1 TBSP | ONION | प्याज़ 4 MEDIUM SIZED / 350 GRAMS (SLICED) | GINGER | अदरक 3 INCH | GARLIC | लहसुन 15-20 CLOVES CLOVES | TOMATO | टमाटर 1 NO. (ROUGHLY CHOPPED) | CORIANDER STEMS | धनिए के डंठल A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) | SALT | नमक A PINCH | HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH | WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED (FOR GRINDING) | OIL | तेल 3-4 TBSP | CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP | BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 NOS. | BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 1 NO. | GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 2 NOS. | CINNAMON | दालचीनी 2 INCH | ONION | प्याज़ 1 NO. (CHOPPED) | CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 15 NOS. | KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TSP | TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP | AGRI MASALA | आगरी मसाला 1 TBSP (You can use spicy Maharashtrian spice mix or a combination of 1 tbsp spicy red chilli powder and 1/2 tsp garam masala instead) | HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED | SALT | नमक TO TASTE | LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF HALF A LEMON | FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) | DESI GHEE | 2 TBSP (Heat the ghee and add it in the end)
instructions: Add the mutton into a large bowl, add all the ingredients of the marinade & mix well, let the mutton marinate until you do the further processes. | You can also add 2 TBSP of curd to the marinade if preferred. | To make the paste, set a pan over high flame, once it gets moderately hot add the dry coconut, stir well & dry roast it over low flame until it turns golden brown, then transfer it into a bowl. | In the same pan add oil, heat it up & add onions, ginger, garlic, stir well & cook over high flame until the onion turns golden brown. | Add tomato, coriander stems, salt, stir well & cook over high flame for 1-2 minutes, then add a splash of hot water, cover & cook over low flame until the tomato turns soft. | Once cooked, transfer it into the same bowl as the dry coconut & let everything cool down to room temperature then grind it into a fine paste using water as required in a mixer grinder. | Set a kadhai over high flame, add oil once it gets hot & let the oil heat up. | Further add all the whole spices & onion, stir well & cook over high flame until the onions start turning golden. | Then lower the flame & add curry leaves, the paste, all the powdered spices along with a splash of water, stir well & cook over high flame until the oil gets separated & the paste becomes crumbly, you can keep adding splashes of hot water if the mixture becomes too dry. | Once the paste gets cooked add the marinated mutton (you can also optionally smoke the mutton using some coal & ghee), stir well & cook the mutton over high flame for 5-6 minutes. | Then cover & let it cook over low flame for a few minutes until the mutton releases its water. | Open the lid & stir well, add more hot water, then cover again & cook over low flame until the mutton gets fully cooked. | Lastly taste the gravy & adjust salt if required & then add lemon juice & fresh coriander, stir well. | Please try to add some hot ghee in the end to finish the curry, tastes incredible! | Your delicious Mutton Curry is ready, serve it with some bhakri or roti or toasted pav.

By the way if you guys don't know, Ajay Sir is a true foodie at heart, he loves to cook for his family and he shared some incredible quick food recipes to try at home and Aaman shared exciting insights from the sets of Azaad, as he gears up for his big-screen debut. We all have watched Ajay sir’s films and love his craft but you will be watching him cook for the first time ever on-screen on YFL and I am sure you would love this side of his. Watch this amazing episode and don’t forget to note down the Ajay Devgn Special Mutton Curry recipe and try it at home! 🎬🔥 Ajay Devgn Mutton Curry Recipe: Prep time: 15-20 minutes Cooking time: 2-2.5 hrs Serves: 4-5 people Ingredients: Marination: MUTTON | मटन 1 KG GINGER GARLIC GREEN CHILLI PASTE | अदरक लहसुन हरी मिर्च की पेस्ट 2 TBSP Salt to taste Turmeric powder | 1/2 TSP Vatan / Paste: DRY COCONUT | सुखा नारियल 1/4 CUP (GRATED) OIL | तेल 1 TBSP ONION | प्याज़ 4 MEDIUM SIZED / 350 GRAMS (SLICED) GINGER | अदरक 3 INCH GARLIC | लहसुन 15-20 CLOVES CLOVES TOMATO | टमाटर 1 NO. (ROUGHLY CHOPPED) CORIANDER STEMS | धनिए के डंठल A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) SALT | नमक A PINCH HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED (FOR GRINDING) Final Cooking: OIL | तेल 3-4 TBSP CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 NOS. BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 1 NO. GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 2 NOS. CINNAMON | दालचीनी 2 INCH ONION | प्याज़ 1 NO. (CHOPPED) CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 15 NOS. POWDERED SPICES: KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TSP TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP AGRI MASALA | आगरी मसाला 1 TBSP (You can use spicy Maharashtrian spice mix or a combination of 1 tbsp spicy red chilli powder and 1/2 tsp garam masala instead) HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED SALT | नमक TO TASTE LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस OF HALF A LEMON FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) DESI GHEE | 2 TBSP (Heat the ghee and add it in the end) Method: Add the mutton into a large bowl, add all the ingredients of the marinade & mix well, let the mutton marinate until you do the further processes. You can also add 2 TBSP of curd to the marinade if preferred. To make the paste, set a pan over high flame, once it gets moderately hot add the dry coconut, stir well & dry roast it over low flame until it turns golden brown, then transfer it into a bowl. In the same pan add oil, heat it up & add onions, ginger, garlic, stir well & cook over high flame until the onion turns golden brown. Add tomato, coriander stems, salt, stir well & cook over high flame for 1-2 minutes, then add a splash of hot water, cover & cook over low flame until the tomato turns soft. Once cooked, transfer it into the same bowl as the dry coconut & let everything cool down to room temperature then grind it into a fine paste using water as required in a mixer grinder. Set a kadhai over high flame, add oil once it gets hot & let the oil heat up. Further add all the whole spices & onion, stir well & cook over high flame until the onions start turning golden. Then lower the flame & add curry leaves, the paste, all the powdered spices along with a splash of water, stir well & cook over high flame until the oil gets separated & the paste becomes crumbly, you can keep adding splashes of hot water if the mixture becomes too dry. Once the paste gets cooked add the marinated mutton (you can also optionally smoke the mutton using some coal & ghee), stir well & cook the mutton over high flame for 5-6 minutes. Then cover & let it cook over low flame for a few minutes until the mutton releases its water. Open the lid & stir well, add more hot water, then cover again & cook over low flame until the mutton gets fully cooked. Lastly taste the gravy & adjust salt if required & then add lemon juice & fresh coriander, stir well. Please try to add some hot ghee in the end to finish the curry, tastes incredible! Your delicious Mutton Curry is ready, serve it with some bhakri or roti or toasted pav. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #AjayDevgn Follow Your Food Lab on: https://www.instagram.com/yourfoodlab/ https://www.facebook.com/yourfoodlab/ Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) https://www.instagram.com/sanjyotkeer/ https://www.facebook.com/sanjyotkeerofficial/ https://x.com/sanjyotkeer The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators)

instructions: Peel the turmeric using a peeler, wear gloves while doing the further processes if preferred because the turmeric will stain your hands. | Once peeled, keep the turmeric in water, then grate it using the large hole grater into a large bowl. | Set a kadhai over high flame, add ghee & let it heat up. | Add the grated turmeric, stir & cook over medium flame until the turmeric changes its colour to golden brown & all the moisture evaporates, make sure that you keep stirring it continuously if you are not using a non-stick kadhai as it has a tendency to stick at the bottom. | Once cooked, transfer the turmeric into a bowl, make sure you press it against the wall of the kadhai you squeeze out the ghee as the further cooking process will happen in the same kadhai. | Add the garlic, ginger & green chilli into a mortar & pound it into a coarse paste, you can also use a small mixer. | Heat the ghee in the kadhai again & add cumin seeds, asafoetida, onion & the ginger garlic chilli paste, stir well & cook over high flame until the onions turn light golden brown. | Add the cashews & cook them for a few seconds. | Add tomato & salt, stir & cook over high flame until the tomatoes turn mushy. | Next, lower the flame & add the powdered spices in the curd & whisk it well, add the whisked spiced curd int the kadhai & stir continuously for a few seconds then increase the flame & cook until the ghee separated. | Add green peas, cooked turmeric, spring onions & cook for 2-3 minutes, you can add a splash of hot water if the gravy is getting too dry, then cover & let the sabzi cook for 4-5 minutes over low flame. | Then the lid off, stir & taste, adjust salt if required & lastly add garam masala & fresh coriander. | Your delicious & comforting haldi ki sabzi is ready, serve it hot with bajra rotlo or sogra or any other millet bread of your choice.

CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/4 TSP ONION | प्याज़ 2 MEDIUM SIZED. (CHOPPED) CASHEW | काजू 10-12 (HALVED) TOMATO | टमाटर 1 NO. (ROUGHLY CHOPPED) CURD | दही 1/2 CUP KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP SPICY RED CHILLI POWDER | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TBSP GREEN PEAS | हरे मटर 1/2 CUP SPRING ONION | हरा प्याज़ A LARGE HANDFUL (ROUGHLY CHOPPED) HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) Method: Peel the turmeric using a peeler, wear gloves while doing the further processes if preferred because the turmeric will stain your hands. Once peeled, keep the turmeric in water, then grate it using the large hole grater into a large bowl. Set a kadhai over high flame, add ghee & let it heat up. Add the grated turmeric, stir & cook over medium flame until the turmeric changes its colour to golden brown & all the moisture evaporates, make sure that you keep stirring it continuously if you are not using a non-stick kadhai as it has a tendency to stick at the bottom. Once cooked, transfer the turmeric into a bowl, make sure you press it against the wall of the kadhai you squeeze out the ghee as the further cooking process will happen in the same kadhai. Add the garlic, ginger & green chilli into a mortar & pound it into a coarse paste, you can also use a small mixer. Heat the ghee in the kadhai again & add cumin seeds, asafoetida, onion & the ginger garlic chilli paste, stir well & cook over high flame until the onions turn light golden brown. Add the cashews & cook them for a few seconds. Add tomato & salt, stir & cook over high flame until the tomatoes turn mushy. Next, lower the flame & add the powdered spices in the curd & whisk it well, add the whisked spiced curd int the kadhai & stir continuously for a few seconds then increase the flame & cook until the ghee separated. Add green peas, cooked turmeric, spring onions & cook for 2-3 minutes, you can add a splash of hot water if the gravy is getting too dry, then cover & let the sabzi cook for 4-5 minutes over low flame. Then the lid off, stir & taste, adjust salt if required & lastly add garam masala & fresh coriander. Your delicious & comforting haldi ki sabzi is ready, serve it hot with bajra rotlo or sogra or any other millet bread of your choice. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #haldikisabji The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Follow us on all platforms: Facebook - https://bit.ly/3o6alLt Instagram - https://bit.ly/2RFkL8z Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) Facebook - https://bit.ly/3tpjqQA​​​​​​​​​ Instagram - https://bit.ly/3o0FZK8 Twitter - https://bit.ly/33qQpJN Intro 0:00 Prepping Turmeric 1:32 Cooking Turmeric 2:31 Final Cooking 4:27 Plating 7:49 Outro 8:51

Next, add the stock or hot water, stir well & bring it to a boil. Then add the chopped veggies, white pepper powder, sugar, light soy sauce, salt & paneer, stir well & cook for 2-3 minutes. Add corn flour & water in a separate bowl & mix well to make a slurry, then add the slurry into the soup while stirring it continuously until the soup thickens. Add the chopped fresh coriander, lemon juice & stir well, then taste & adjust salt if required, you can also add more lemon juice if required. Lastly add spring onion greens & your comforting & delicious lemon coriander soup is ready. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #VegLemonCorianderSoup The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Follow us on all platforms: Facebook - https://bit.ly/3o6alLt Instagram - https://bit.ly/2RFkL8z Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) Facebook - https://bit.ly/3tpjqQA​​​​​​​​​ Instagram - https://bit.ly/3o0FZK8 Twitter - https://bit.ly/33qQpJN Intro 0:00 Prep 1:05 Quick Stock 2:47 Soup 3:47 Plating 6:00 Outro 6:33

GARLIC | लहसुन 4 CLOVES GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH (ROUGHLY CHOPPED) MUSTARD OIL | सरसों का तेल 2 TBSP MUSTARD SEEDS | राई ½ TSP CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा ½ TSP RED CHILLI | लाल मिर्च 1 NO. ASAFOETIDA | हींग ¼ tsp CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 8-10 LEAVES GINGER GARLIC GREEN CHILLI PASTE SALT | नमक TO TASTE BLACK SALT | काला नमकA PINCH BLACK PEPPER | काली मिर्च A PINCH (FRESHLY CRUSHED) TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर ¼ TSP GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस 1 TSP FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED) Method: Wash the beans & pat them dry, then cut the tops and the bottom slightly & chop them into 1-2 cm thick dices. Cut the peeled potatoes into dices & make a coarse past of ginger, garlic & green chilli using a mortar & pestle. Set a kadhai over high flame, add the mustard oil & let it heat up until it reaches its smoking point, then lower the flame & let the oil cool down slightly. Then add mustard & cumin seeds, red chilli, asafoetida, curry leaves & the ginger garlic paste, stir well & cook over high flame briefly. Next, add the diced potatoes, stir well & cook over high flame for 1-2 minutes, then cover & cook over low flame until the potatoes get 90% cooked, take the lid off & stir the potatoes at intervals, this process will take 7-8 minutes. Once the potatoes are 90% cooked, add the beans along with salt, black salt, black pepper & turmeric powder, stir well then cover & cook over low flame for 5-6 minutes or until the beans get cook, I prefer them to be a little crunchy but you can cook them further until soft. Give it a stir 2-3 times while cooking just to ensure that the potatoes don’t stick to the bottom. Once cooked, add garam masala, lemon juice & fresh coriander, stir well & your quick & easy Aloo Beans sabzi is ready.
